item.title: Mid-Summer AC Service: Keep Your Cool Through Georgia’s Hottest Months
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve lived in Winder for more than a summer, you know what’s coming. It’s the heat and humidity of July in Georgia. It’s not just hot, it’s relentless. And if your air conditioner isn’t in top shape, this time of year can turn your home into a sauna. That’s why a mid-summer AC service isn’t just helpful, it’s essential.

Even if your system has been working since the spring, July is when it’s put to the ultimate test. Don’t wait for signs of trouble to appear. Here’s why now is the perfect time to schedule AC maintenance, and how it can help you stay cool, efficient, and stress-free through the worst of the summer heat.

Why Mid-Summer Is the Most Demanding Time for Your AC

In Winder and across much of Georgia, July is typically the hottest month of the year. Daily highs often push into the 90s, and the humidity only makes it feel worse. Your air conditioner is running more frequently, for longer stretches of time, and often around the clock.

All that extra work can lead to:

  • Increased wear and tear on parts like capacitors, motors, and fans
  • Reduced cooling efficiency
  • Higher energy bills
  • A greater risk of system breakdowns

If your system hasn’t had a check-up since spring–or worse, since last year–it’s time to give it the attention it needs to keep working when you need it most.

Benefits of Mid-Summer AC Service

A mid-summer AC tune-up is like a wellness check for your cooling system. It keeps everything running smoothly and can save you from bigger problems down the road. Here’s what you gain from scheduling service now:

  • Catch Small Issues Before They Become Big Repairs: A technician can spot early signs of trouble, like refrigerant leaks, weak airflow, or dirty coils, before they turn into full system failures.
  • Improve Energy Efficiency: Cleaning and inspecting components like air filters, condenser coils, and blower fans can help your system run more efficiently, saving you money on cooling costs.
  • Maximize Indoor Comfort: If your system has been struggling to keep up with the heat, a tune-up can help it cool your home more effectively and evenly.
  • Extend the Life of Your AC: Just like changing the oil in your car, regular maintenance helps your AC last longer and perform better over time.

What’s Included in a Mid-Summer AC Tune-Up?

A typical AC service visit from a trusted local HVAC professional includes:

  • Checking refrigerant levels and pressures
  • Cleaning or replacing the air filter
  • Inspecting and cleaning the evaporator and condenser coils
  • Tightening electrical connections
  • Testing the thermostat
  • Lubricating moving parts
  • Measuring airflow and checking for blockages
  • Ensuring proper condensate drainage

If any issues are found, your technician can recommend the right repairs or adjustments to prevent future problems. Even if everything seems to be running fine, proactive maintenance gives you peace of mind that your system is ready for whatever Georgia’s summer throws your way.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how AC maintenance can help you stay cool, efficient, and stress-free through the worst of the summer heat.

item.title: Signs Your AC Needs Repair Before the Summer Heat Hits Hard
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
As summer approaches in Winder, Georgia, reliable air conditioning becomes more than a luxury—it’s a necessity. Waiting until your system breaks down during a sweltering July afternoon isn’t just inconvenient; it can be downright miserable.

That’s why identifying early warning signs of AC trouble before the summer heat hits hard is essential for your comfort, safety, and budget. Here are the most common red flags that your air conditioner might need repair soon—and why it’s smart to take action now.

1. Weak or Inconsistent Airflow

If you notice that some rooms in your home aren’t cooling as well as others, or the air coming from your vents feels faint or sluggish, your AC may be struggling to circulate air effectively. This can point to several potential problems, including:

  • Clogged air filters
  • Ductwork obstructions or leaks
  • Failing blower motor
  • Low refrigerant levels

Ignoring weak airflow can cause your system to work harder, increase energy consumption, and lead to more costly repairs if not addressed quickly.

2. Unusual Sounds from the Unit

Air conditioners typically make a gentle hum when running, but if you hear grinding, squealing, rattling, or banging noises, it’s a sure sign something isn’t right. These sounds could indicate:

  • Loose or damaged components
  • Worn-out belts
  • Motor or compressor issues

Strange noises are often an early warning that a part is failing. Catching the issue early can prevent further damage and a mid-summer breakdown.

3. Spikes in Energy Bills

Have your electric bills been creeping up without a clear reason? If your usage hasn’t changed but your utility costs have, your air conditioner may be losing efficiency. Common causes include:

  • Dirty coils
  • Low refrigerant
  • Failing components that cause your system to run longer

An inefficient system not only costs you more monthly—it may be heading toward failure. Scheduling an inspection before temperatures rise can save you money and stress.

4. Warm Air or Inconsistent Cooling

If your AC is running but not blowing cool air—or if the temperature throughout your home fluctuates dramatically—it may signal refrigerant leaks, thermostat problems, or issues with the compressor. Consistent cooling is key in Winder’s hot and humid summers, and any irregularity should be investigated before you’re relying on your system full-time.

5. Foul Odors or Musty Smells

If your air conditioning system is emitting unpleasant smells, such as musty, burning, or chemical-like odors, it could indicate:

  • Mold or mildew in the ductwork or unit
  • Electrical problems
  • Refrigerant leaks

A professional cleaning or repair might be needed to restore indoor air quality and ensure your system is operating safely.

6. Frequent Cycling or Extended Run Times

Is your system turning on and off more frequently than usual? Or running longer without reaching the set temperature? These symptoms may be related to:

  • Oversized or undersized systems
  • Dirty filters
  • Thermostat malfunctions
  • Failing components

These issues strain your system and increase wear and tear, which can shorten the lifespan of your AC.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for HVAC service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn the most common red flags that your air conditioner might need repair soon—and why it’s smart to take action now.

item.title: Breathe Easier This Summer: Why June Is the Perfect Time for Air Duct Cleaning in Winder, GA
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
As summer settles in and the heat rises, homeowners across the region begin to rely heavily on their air conditioning systems. June marks the start of the hot and humid season, where indoor comfort becomes essential – not just for staying cool, but also for maintaining a healthy and breathable living environment. 

One often-overlooked way to enhance both comfort and air quality is by scheduling air duct cleaning. If you’ve never had your air ducts professionally cleaned, or if it’s been several years since your last service, June is the ideal time to schedule this important maintenance task. Here’s why.

The Role of Air Ducts in Your Home’s Comfort and Health

Your home’s ductwork serves as the central highway for heated or cooled air. It circulates air from your HVAC system into every room, which means it also distributes whatever is lingering inside the ducts – including dust, pollen, pet dander, mold spores, and even bacteria.

Over time, these contaminants can accumulate in the ductwork. Without proper cleaning, the buildup can:

  • Reduce airflow efficiency
  • Aggravate allergies or respiratory conditions
  • Cause unpleasant odors
  • Strain your HVAC system, leading to higher energy bills

Why June Is the Best Time for Air Duct Cleaning in Winder

There are several reasons why homeowners in Winder should consider air duct cleaning specifically during the month of June:

1. Peak HVAC Usage Is Right Around the Corner

As temperatures climb into the high 80s and 90s, your air conditioner will be working hard every day. Dirty ducts make it harder for your system to cool your home efficiently. By cleaning your air ducts in June, you ensure optimal airflow just before the most demanding part of the summer.

2. Allergy Season in Georgia Is in Full Swing

In Winder, June often brings elevated pollen levels along with humidity – two factors that worsen allergy symptoms. If pollen, dust, and mold spores have collected in your ducts over the spring, running your AC will simply recirculate those irritants throughout your home. Air duct cleaning removes these allergens and provides cleaner, fresher air indoors.

3. Prevent Mold Growth from Humid Air

Georgia summers are known for their high humidity, which creates a perfect environment for mold growth in dark, damp spaces like ductwork. Cleaning your ducts before the most humid part of the season helps remove any existing mold and prevents it from spreading through your air supply.

The Benefits of Professional Air Duct Cleaning

Beyond seasonal advantages, air duct cleaning offers year-round benefits that improve both health and HVAC performance.

  • Improved indoor air quality: Removing dust, debris, and contaminants leads to cleaner, healthier air for your family to breathe.
  • Better energy efficiency: Clean ducts allow air to flow more freely, helping your AC cool faster and run less frequently.
  • Extended HVAC lifespan: When your system doesn’t have to work as hard, it experiences less wear and tear, potentially extending its service life.
  • Elimination of musty odors: Air ducts can trap smells from pets, cooking, and mold. Cleaning them helps remove these lingering odors from your home.

Take Action Before the Summer Heat Peaks

Don’t wait for hot, stagnant air and rising energy bills to remind you that your HVAC system needs attention. By scheduling air duct cleaning in June, you prepare your home for a clean, cool, and comfortable summer.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for duct services. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn why June is the ideal time to schedule this important maintenance task.

item.title: New Refrigerants Are Here! Benefits of the Update
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
As of 2025, the HVAC industry has officially transitioned to a new generation of refrigerants, ushering in a major shift in how residential air conditioning systems are built, serviced, and evaluated for performance. 

If you’re considering upgrading your HVAC system or have an older unit that’s showing signs of wear, now is the perfect time to explore the benefits of the latest refrigerant technology. Not only are these new systems better for the environment, but they’re also designed to be significantly more efficient—helping homeowners enjoy better comfort and lower energy bills for years to come.

What’s Changing in 2025?

Beginning in January 2025, new federal guidelines require that all newly manufactured residential HVAC systems use refrigerants with a lower global warming potential (GWP). This shift is part of the AIM Act (American Innovation and Manufacturing Act), which aims to phase down the use of older hydrofluorocarbon (HFC) refrigerants—such as R-410A—due to their environmental impact.

The leading replacement refrigerant in most residential systems is R-454B, which has a GWP approximately 78% lower than R-410A. This new refrigerant complies with environmental standards while maintaining excellent performance in high-efficiency air conditioning and heat pump systems.

Benefits of the New Refrigerants

Upgrading to a new HVAC system that uses R-454B or similar next-generation refrigerants offers several advantages:

  • Improved system efficiency: Modern HVAC systems using updated refrigerants are engineered to deliver higher Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio 2 (SEER2) ratings. This means your home will stay cooler with less energy consumption, leading to lower monthly utility bills.
  • Enhanced environmental responsibility: By choosing a unit with a low-GWP refrigerant, you’re directly contributing to global efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. It’s a small change with a big impact.
  • Future-proofing your investment: Systems that rely on R-410A will become more expensive to service over time as the refrigerant becomes less available and more tightly regulated. By upgrading now, you avoid rising costs and ensure easier maintenance down the line.
  • Quieter, smarter operation: Today’s systems are built for efficiency and comfort, offering variable-speed compressors, smart thermostat compatibility, and quieter performance.

Why Now Is the Time to Replace

If your system is over 10 years old or frequently requires repairs, now is an ideal time to make the switch. Not only will you benefit from the new refrigerant standards and technology, but you can also take advantage of current promotions and incentives that make the transition more affordable.

Take Advantage of Our Current System Replacement Offer

For a limited time, we’re offering exclusive deals on new HVAC installations that meet the 2025 refrigerant standards. These offers include:

  • Special financing options with approved credit
  • Manufacturer rebates on qualifying high-efficiency systems
  • Professional installation by certified technicians familiar with the new refrigerant guidelines
  • Extended warranty coverage for added peace of mind

By acting now, you’ll secure a high-performance system that complies with upcoming regulations, reduces your energy costs, and delivers reliable comfort for years to come.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for HVAC service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn the benefits of the latest refrigerant technology.

item.title: Ductless is a Great Option for Cooling, But What About Heating?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Ductless mini-split systems have exploded in popularity in recent years—and for good reason. They offer flexible, efficient cooling for homes of all shapes and sizes, without the need for traditional ductwork. But if you’ve been considering a ductless system, you might be wondering: Is it a good option for heating too?

The short answer: Absolutely. Keep reading to learn how ductless systems are not only a smart cooling solution, but also an incredibly effective and energy-efficient way to heat your home—even during colder months.

How Ductless Systems Work for Heating and Cooling

A ductless mini-split system operates using a heat pump technology. That means it can both cool and heat your home depending on the season. Instead of generating heat by burning fuel like a traditional furnace, a ductless heat pump moves heat—either pulling it from inside your home to cool it (summer mode) or pulling it from the outdoor air to warm your home (winter mode).

Even when outdoor temperatures drop, there’s still heat energy available in the air. Modern ductless systems are designed to extract that heat efficiently and transfer it indoors, keeping you warm without the massive energy costs associated with older heating methods.

The Heating Benefits of a Ductless System

1. Energy Efficiency – Ductless mini-split systems are among the most energy-efficient HVAC options available. Many models have extremely high SEER (Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio) ratings for cooling and impressive HSPF (Heating Seasonal Performance Factor) ratings for heating. Without the energy losses that come from ductwork leaks—common in older homes—ductless systems deliver almost all the heating energy they generate directly into your living spaces.

2. Zone Control and Personalized Comfort – Each indoor unit operates independently, allowing you to customize the temperature in each room or zone. This zoned heating approach eliminates the need to heat the entire home when you’re only using a few rooms, resulting in lower energy usage and lower heating bills.

3. Reliable Cold-Weather Performance – Today’s advanced ductless heat pumps are engineered to perform even in low outdoor temperatures. Some models, known as “cold climate heat pumps,” are specifically designed to maintain strong heating performance well below freezing. For homeowners in areas with moderate to chilly winters, ductless heating offers reliable and consistent comfort.

4. Easy Installation and Flexibility – Because they don’t require ductwork, ductless systems are perfect for homes without existing ducts, home additions, converted garages, or anywhere adding new ductwork would be impractical or expensive. Installation is often faster and less invasive compared to traditional systems.

5. Improved Indoor Air Quality – Without ducts to collect dust, allergens, and other pollutants, ductless systems can actually help improve your home’s air quality. Many ductless units also come with built-in filtration systems to further clean the air as they heat or cool your rooms.

Are There Any Limitations?

While ductless systems are incredibly versatile, they may not be the best fit for every situation. Extremely large homes with extensive heating needs might require more indoor units or a combination of systems, ultimately increasing the cost of upfront installation. 

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for HVAC service. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how ductless systems are not only a smart cooling solution, but also an incredibly effective and energy-efficient way to heat your home—even during colder months.

item.title: What&#8217;s the Best Option for a Water Heater Upgrade
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
If you’re ready to upgrade your water heater, understanding the different types—tank vs. tankless, gas vs. electric vs. heat pump—can help you make the best choice for your home. Upgrading your water heater is one of the smartest moves you can make as a homeowner. 

A new water heater can increase your home’s energy efficiency, lower utility bills, and ensure you never run out of hot water when you need it most. But with so many options on the market, how do you know which type is right for your household? Let’s break down the most common types of water heaters and highlight their benefits so you can make a confident, informed decision.

Traditional Tank Water Heaters

Best for: Households with consistent hot water use and a tighter upfront budget

Tank water heaters are the most familiar option. They store a set amount of hot water—typically 40 to 80 gallons—that’s ready when you need it. When the hot water runs out, the tank refills and heats again. Benefits of tank water heaters include:

  • Lower Initial Cost: Tank models are generally less expensive to purchase and install compared to tankless units.
  • Reliable Supply: Provides a steady and reliable volume of hot water for daily use.
  • Fewer Installation Hurdles: Most homes are already set up for tank models, making replacements simpler.

While tank water heaters may cost more to run over time due to standby heat loss (keeping water hot even when it’s not being used), newer models are more efficient than ever and may still suit many homeowners.

Tankless Water Heaters

Best for: Energy-conscious homeowners or households with fluctuating hot water needs

Tankless water heaters, also called on-demand systems, heat water only when you turn on the tap. Instead of storing water, they use high-powered burners or electric elements to deliver hot water instantly. Benefits of tankless water heaters include:

  • Energy Efficiency: Since they don’t store water, there’s no energy wasted keeping water hot 24/7.
  • Endless Hot Water: Great for larger families or homes with high hot water demand.
  • Space Saving: These compact units are mounted on a wall, freeing up floor space.
  • Longer Lifespan: Tankless units typically last 20+ years, compared to 10–15 for tank models.

Keep in mind that tankless systems usually require a larger upfront investment and may need upgrades to your home’s electrical or gas lines.

Heat Pump Water Heaters

Best for: Homeowners seeking maximum efficiency and long-term savings

Heat pump water heaters use electricity—but rather than generating heat directly, they move heat from the air into the water tank. They’re ideal for moderate to warm climates and work best in garages or utility spaces. Benefits of heat pump water heaters:

  • High efficiency: they use up to 60% less energy than traditional electric models.
  • Lower Energy Bills: You’ll see savings on your monthly utility bills thanks to reduced electricity use.
  • Environmentally Friendly: Ideal for homeowners looking to reduce their carbon footprint.

Find the Right Fit for Your Home

Choosing the best water heater comes down to your household’s hot water habits, energy preferences, space availability, and long-term budget. Whether you want the reliability of a traditional tank, the efficiency of a heat pump, or the luxury of endless hot water from a tankless system, there’s a solution that’s just right for your home.

Frequently Asked Questions

Question: What are the main types of water heaters available for an upgrade?
Answer: The most common options include traditional tank water heaters, tankless water heaters, and heat pump (hybrid) models. Tank units are budget-friendly, tankless provide endless hot water, and heat pump systems offer excellent efficiency, especially in warm climates.

Question: What are the advantages of choosing a tankless water heater?
Answer: Tankless water heaters heat water on demand, which means no standby heat loss and a steady supply of hot water. They are compact and long-lasting, but they usually cost more upfront and may require electrical or gas line upgrades.

Question: How do heat pump water heaters compare in terms of efficiency?
Answer: Heat pump water heaters move heat from the surrounding air into the water, making them much more efficient than traditional electric units. They can cut energy use significantly, which lowers monthly utility costs, and are best suited for moderate to warm climates.

Question: When might a traditional tank water heater still be the best option?
Answer: A traditional tank water heater is often the best choice if you want a lower initial cost, need consistent hot water, or if your home is already set up for a tank system. Although less efficient, they remain reliable and practical for many households.

Question: How should I choose the right water heater for my household?
Answer: Choosing the right water heater depends on your budget, energy goals, and hot water usage. Tank models are affordable and simple, tankless units save space and provide endless hot water, while heat pump systems are ideal if efficiency and lower energy bills are your priorities.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn about the most common types of water heaters and highlight their benefits so you can make a confident, informed decision.

item.title: How to Tell if It&#8217;s Time for a Heat Pump Upgrade
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When your heat pump is working the way it should, it provides reliable comfort throughout the year—heating in the winter and cooling in the summer. But even the best systems can wear down over time. If you’ve been dealing with rising utility bills, noisy operation, or uneven comfort in your home, your heat pump may be trying to tell you something. 

We help homeowners determine the right time to upgrade to a newer, more efficient unit. Keep reading to learn the top signs it’s time to consider a heat pump replacement and how upgrading can benefit your home. Need expert advice? Contact our team to schedule a professional consultation and keep your home running efficiently year-round.

1. Your Heat Pump Is More Than 10 to 15 Years Old

Age is one of the biggest indicators that it’s time for a replacement. Most heat pumps are designed to last around 10 to 15 years with regular maintenance. Once your system crosses that threshold, it begins to lose efficiency, experience more frequent breakdowns, and cost more to operate. If you’re nearing that range, it’s a good time to start evaluating your options.

2. You’re Spending More on Repairs

If you’ve had to schedule multiple repairs over the past year—or if one major repair costs more than half the price of a new unit—it may be more cost-effective to replace the entire system. Frequent repairs can indicate that your unit is wearing down overall, and those costs can quickly add up. 

3. Your Energy Bills Are Climbing

An aging heat pump has to work harder to heat or cool your home, and that inefficiency shows up in your monthly energy bills. If you notice your utility bills are steadily increasing despite consistent usage, your system could be losing performance due to wear and tear or outdated technology.

4. Your Comfort Isn’t Consistent

Are some rooms too hot while others remain cold? Do you feel like your system constantly runs but never reaches the desired temperature? Uneven temperatures and longer run times are signs that your heat pump is no longer distributing air properly—or that it’s undersized or outdated for your current home needs.

5. You’re Interested in Better Efficiency & Smart Features

New heat pumps are more efficient than ever, with features like variable-speed compressors, quiet operation, and smart thermostat compatibility. If your current unit lacks these options, you could be missing out on better comfort and savings.

6. Considering a Heat Pump for Environmental Benefits

Heat pumps are energy-efficient by design and offer an eco-friendly way to heat and cool your home without relying on fossil fuels. Upgrading to a modern unit with a high SEER2 (Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio 2) or HSPF2 (Heating Seasonal Performance Factor 2) rating can help reduce your carbon footprint.

7. Our Experts Make It Easy

When it’s time to upgrade, our licensed technicians will walk you through the best replacement options based on your home size, comfort goals, and budget. We provide full-service installation, financing options, and ongoing support to make the entire process smooth and stress-free.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heat pump service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn the top signs it’s time to consider a heat pump replacement and how upgrading can benefit your home.

item.title: Take Care of Heater Repairs Before the Season Is Over
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
As the season transitions and temperatures begin to drop, it is essential to ensure your heating system is operating efficiently. If you need reliable heating repair in Lawrenceville, GA, addressing repairs before the end of the current season is a wise decision. 

Keep reading to learn why timely heater repairs are critical for maintaining a warm, safe home environment. Then give us a call to get your services on our schedule before turning your unit off for the spring and summer seasons. It’s a great way to be proactive and ensure your heater is in great working order for later this year.

Early Problem Detection and Repair

Regular inspections allow you to detect small issues before they become major problems. Common issues such as worn-out components, minor gas leaks, or faulty thermostats can be identified early through scheduled maintenance. 

Addressing these issues promptly not only prevents costly emergency repairs later on but also ensures your heater runs safely and efficiently throughout the season. Early repairs also help avoid the discomfort of sudden heating failures on cold days.

Enhanced Energy Efficiency and Cost Savings

A well-maintained heating system operates more efficiently, reducing energy consumption and lowering utility bills. When components like burners, heat exchangers, and electrical connections are in optimal condition, the system doesn’t have to work as hard to deliver consistent warmth. Regular maintenance and timely repairs optimize your system’s performance, leading to both immediate energy savings and long-term cost benefits.

Safety and Reliability Considerations

Safety is paramount when it comes to heating systems, especially those that rely on gas. Faulty equipment or neglected maintenance can pose significant safety hazards, including the risk of carbon monoxide leaks or even fires. 

Professional heater repair includes comprehensive safety checks that ensure your system meets all operational standards. This proactive approach not only keeps your home warm but also provides peace of mind in knowing that your family is protected against potential hazards.

Long-Term System Longevity

Investing in regular heater repairs and maintenance extends the lifespan of your heating system. Over time, even a high-quality unit will experience wear and tear. It can age quickly and need to be replaced sooner, or it can age slower and last longer. 

By keeping up with repairs and preventive maintenance, you safeguard your investment and delay the need for a costly system replacement. A reliable, long-lasting heater contributes to overall home comfort and maintains consistent performance throughout the colder months.

Additional Considerations for an Energy-Efficient Home

Beyond repairs, consider complementary measures to ensure your entire home is more energy-efficient. This might include insulating pipes, sealing drafts, and scheduling a full HVAC system evaluation. Combining these steps with timely heater repairs creates a comprehensive approach that maximizes comfort and efficiency. With a well-rounded maintenance plan, you’ll enjoy a warm, safe, and energy-efficient home all season long.

item.title: Tips to Care for Your Tankless Water Heater
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Having a tankless water heater is a huge benefit when it comes to having a nearly endless supply of hot water. But these unique systems do require some extra care steps that you don’t have to complete when you have a more traditional tank water heater.

Of course, aside from steps that you complete as a homeowner, it is also important to engage our team for tankless water heater maintenance in Winder, GA once each year. We are here to help with all of your needs big and small – so give us a call anytime. In the meantime, you can keep reading to learn about the ways you can care for your tankless water heater in between those professional service appointments. 

Schedule an Annual Flush 

If your home’s water source is high in mineral content, that means you have hard water. These minerals are especially tough on tankless water heaters since the setup has smaller lines. Mineral buildup is abrasive, so it can lead to corrosion. But it also causes limescale buildup that can block water flow. 

To prevent these problems it’s a good idea to flush the system annually while a technician cleans it. This is something our team can do during an annual maintenance appointment to help your water heater work at its absolute best. 

Check for Error Codes 

If you have an older water heater it may not be computerized to display error codes. But many modern tankless water heaters do have a feature to help you identify problems earlier rather than later. You can compare the error code to the user manual to see what is wrong. If you’re not sure what to do, give us a call. We can offer you guidance or visit your home for a service appointment.

Older water heaters might show issues by giving off foul odors or strange noises. Instead of an error code, if your water heater is an older model that doesn’t have this kind of technology, keep your ears peeled for any noises that make you uncomfortable.

Monitor Water Pressure 

Your water pressure should be just right – not too strong and not too weak. If your hot water pressure seems excessively strong, it can cause a lot of undue strain on the system. Your tankless water heater may begin to leak or show signs of serious damage. A pressure regulator is an add-on option that can help protect your unit by displaying water pressure readings for you to easily see. 

Invest in Insulation

Your tankless water heater lines may be prone to freezing in the wintertime when temperatures dip below freezing. If your lines aren’t already insulated, invest in a kit that can protect your tankless water heater during freeze warnings. You can insulate the lines when you need to and then remove the added insulation when freeze warnings are over and temperatures warm back up.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn about the ways you can care for your tankless water heater in between those professional service appointments.

item.title: Hot and Cold: How Your Shower May Be Signaling Time for a New Water Heater
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes it can seem like plumbing problems don’t offer any signs until they are serious. But oftentimes, there are signs and you just have to know what to look for. Today we’re going to talk about how your shower may be telling you that there are plumbing problems needing to be addressed. 

If you notice any of the signs below, give our team a call to schedule an assessment for water heater installation in Winder. You can also keep reading to learn how a new water heater may be the difference maker in eliminating some common plumbing problems.

Lack of Heat

If your water is not getting as hot as you would like for it to be during a shower, then it may be time for a new water heater. Whether your model is gas or electric, the elements creating heat may be aging and wearing out. It’s also possible that mineral buildup from hard water is blocking your heating element from being able to increase the temperature of water enough. 

Ideally your water heater should not be set higher than 120°F. You may be tempted to turn up the temperature as a way of boosting heat, but we recommend against this. Instead, your water heater needs to be flushed out to see if that helps. If not, it may be time for a total replacement.

Inconsistent Temperatures

If you are taking a shower, then the water should remain a consistent temperature until hot water runs out because of the tank supply. If the water seems to fluctuate between being hotter and cooler without you making adjustments to the faucet, your water heater may be to blame. Your water heater is designed to push tap water entering the tank down through the dip tube to the bottom to be heated up. 

This ensures that the water coming through your shower is consistently hot. A malfunctioning dip tube can sometimes be fixed, but there’s also a chance that your water heater simply needs to be replaced if it is already older. 

Water heaters can last for anywhere between 8 and 15 years depending on how well they are cared for and whether they are gas or electric. Keep in mind that maintaining your water heater through consistent professional care every year we go a long way toward helping it last longer.

Changes in Water Pressure

If it seems like water pressure goes down the hotter that you turn your faucet, there may be an issue with the water coming from your water heater. Of course, it is important to consider that if you have lower water pressure overall it is probably something else wrong. 

However, noticing a significant difference between the pressure of cold water versus hot water, usually points toward water heater issues specifically. Just like the other concerns we listed above, our team can check out your system to see if it can be repaired, and if not we can make recommendations for installing a replacement.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how a new water heater may be the difference maker in eliminating some common plumbing problems.

item.title: Heater Maintenance Tips for Better Operation
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Most of the homeowners that we partner with for HVAC service agree that they want their heaters to work as well as possible. Aside from scheduling professional service, there are a few care tips you can take into consideration to help your heater work at its best. 

Keep reading to learn everything you need to know about heater maintenance in the winter season. Then give our team a call when you’re ready to schedule your annual heating maintenance service appointment.

Air Filter Selection

Your first step is to choose an air filter that meets the needs of your heater. This means choosing a filter that is a perfect fit for the intake grate without being too big or too small. You also want to pay attention to the MERV ratings on air filters and choose one that will clean your indoor air without blocking airflow. 

We typically recommend an air filter with a MERV rating somewhere between eight and 13. Having the right air filter in place protects your heater from harmful particles that can create a buildup inside of the system and interfere with operation.

Air Filter Care

It’s equally important to change your air filter out in a timely manner. Whether your filter is rated to last one month or three months, it’s important to change it out on time before it gets too full. Otherwise, dust and dirt may begin seeping through the filter and getting into your heater. These tiny particles can get in between intricate gears and dry up lubrication that helps your system to operate effectively.

Thermostat Settings

You can also take care of your heater by using reasonable thermostat settings. If you set the temperature too high, your heating system won’t be able to keep up with heating your home effectively. This will add a lot of strain to the unit. Instead, set your thermostat no higher than around 68° for heating. This ensures that your system can heat your home adequately without taking on extra wear and tear thanks to strain from working harder. 

Cleaning the Cabinet

It is inevitable that some dust will settle onto the heating cabinet. Once a month, you can turn your heater off completely and give it time to cool down. Then you can wipe down the outside of the cabinet. We don’t recommend opening the furnace up since that can be a safety concern. Our team can be the one to maintain and clean the inside of your furnace.

Vacuum and Dust

You probably think of vacuuming and dusting as household chores. However, these chores are also benefiting your heater. When there is less dust and dirt in your home to begin with, there is less that can get into the air and into your heating system.

This can help your heater work a little more efficiently and also reduce the chances of needing to schedule any type of professional service aside from regular heating maintenance in Winder.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn everything you need to know about heater maintenance in winter season.

item.title: 4 Signs of Commercial Heater Problems
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Some heater problems are easy to identify while others are quiet or nearly silent. But even quiet problems do offer signs that something is going wrong and needs to be addressed. You can keep reading to learn about four common signs of heating problems at a business.

Then give us a call to schedule services for your commercial heating in Lawrenceville, GA. Whether a heater problem is obvious or you just have a gut feeling that something is wrong, a service appointment can offer you peace of mind and save you a headache later on.

1. Inconsistent Heating

One early sign of commercial heater problems is experiencing inconsistent temperatures across the different spaces in your building. Some rooms or areas may feel perfectly warm, while others are cooler or even cold. With a home, this problem is most likely caused by ductwork damage, but business HVAC systems are more complex. 

If you have multiple HVAC units connected together there may be a problem with a single unit, a malfunctioning thermostat, clogged air filters, or indeed the ductwork. Instead of trying to adjust air vents to change airflow, we encourage you to call our team for service. We can get down to the root cause of the problem to optimize performance and prevent further issues.

2. Unusual Sounds 

A commercial heater should usually operate fairly quietly with minimal sound from the blower or air moving through vents. Hearing unusual sounds that your unit doesn’t usually make is a red flag. Pay attention to noises like:

  • Banging
  • Rattling
  • Squealing
  • Hissing
  • Booming

These noises can be caused by any number of issues from loose or damaged components to a failing motor or imbalanced pressure. You may be tempted to ignore these sounds if your heater is working normally; otherwise, doing so can lead to more significant problems.

3. High Energy Costs

A sudden spike in the cost of your energy bills could be caused by operational problems. If you haven’t done something to increase your business’s energy consumption, you shouldn’t see major changes in cost. 

High energy costs can be caused by anything from clogged filters that are blocking airflow to components that are wearing out and beginning to malfunction. One example of this would be a belt that is loosening up or gears that are grinding thanks to grimy buildup. 

4. Frequent Cycling 

Short cycling is something that happens when your commercial heater turns on and off more frequently than normal, usually every couple of minutes. The unit never stays on long enough to change the temperature inside your business and consumes more energy even though it isn’t heating your space. 

The frequent on and off of short cycling adds a lot of strain to the components of your commercial heater. It may need repairs more frequently, break down unexpectedly, or need to be replaced sooner than you expect. Don’t delay – schedule service if you suspect this problem or any of the others above.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn about four common signs of heating problems at a business.

item.title: 3 Common Holiday Repair Needs for Furnaces&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
The holidays are filled with joyful times and busy days. Furnace problems can throw a wrench into the most well laid plans, so staying ahead of repair needs is essential at this time of the year. You can keep reading to learn about the early signs of three common furnace repair needs that we get calls about at this time of the year.

By identifying a problem early on, you can prevent a total breakdown from happening. If you notice any of these signs, give our team a call right away to schedule furnace repair in Gainesville, GA. That way you can schedule service on your terms instead of putting off an appointment and potentially being left without any heat.

Thermostat Issues

Did you know that sometimes furnace problems are not actually furnace problems at all? Sometimes your furnace is malfunctioning, but the thermostat is to blame. If it seems like your furnace is turning on and off frequently, or turning on and staying on, the thermostat may be to blame. 

It’s possible that the sensor is reading the indoor air temperature incorrectly or that it is not communicating effectively with your furnace. We can troubleshoot with your thermostat to pinpoint the exact problem and get it fixed. 

Overheating

If your furnace never stays on long enough to heat up your home, or seems like it’s not turning on at all, there may be an issue with it overheating. This can happen when any number of things inside of the system goes wrong and causes the unit to work hard harder than it needs to. 

Electrical problems can contribute to overheating, but so can heat getting trapped inside of the system. Heat gets trapped when there is a blockage preventing airflow from blowing out into your home, or because the blower motor is broken. No matter what the cause of overheating, our team can check it out and fix the component that is the culprit.

Cracked Heat Exchanger

This problem is exclusive to gas furnaces, but it is worth mentioning since it can be an extremely serious problem. The heat exchanger is where gas releases to meet the pilot light and combusts to create heat for your home. The chamber is completely airtight and has a precise process to ensure that clean heated air moves in one direction while the carbon monoxide from gas moves in another direction away from your home. 

Strain can lead the heat exchanger to crack as time goes on. If this happens, carbon monoxide gas can leak out into your home. The biggest problem with this is that gas does not have an odor, making a leak difficult to detect. If you notice any type of damage to the heat exchanger of your gas furnace, call for service right away. 

If you have any concerns with how your gas furnace is operating, turn it off completely and let us take a look at it before you turn it back on again. Remember that carbon monoxide detectors should be in place when you have gas service established at your home, whether you have a gas furnace or not.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heater service in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn about the early signs of three common furnace repair needs that we get calls about at this time of the year.

item.title: What Does Air Duct Cleaning Do For Your Home?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Have you been thinking about ways to boost your indoor air quality? If so, now is a great time to make such an investment as we head into the winter season. Indoor air quality is known to be poorer in the winter time because we tend to keep our homes closed up. 

You can keep reading to learn more about why services like air duct cleaning can positively impact your indoor air quality this winter season. Air duct cleaning in Winder, GA is a great way to breathe easier and protect your health this holiday season.

Reduce Dust Levels

Dust builds up inside of your ductwork very slowly over the years. This happens in a couple of different ways. First, particles get into your system through the intake grate because even when you have an excellent air filter in place, it is inevitable for some particles to get through little by little. 

It may not be much, but the dust can add up very quickly as years go by. Dust can also get into the ductwork as the seals around individual ductwork pieces wear out. As seals loosen, dust can get into your ductwork. The problem is exacerbated as dust weakens the seals further, creating bigger holes and allowing even more dust into the system. 

It’s a cycle that is only broken by getting a fresh start with duct cleaning services. Getting a fresh start means that fewer dust particles are mixing in to the clean air and blowing back out into your home. 

Improve Health Outcomes 

When you invest in duct cleaning services once every three to five years, you eliminate harmful particles that you would otherwise breathe in. Dust is made up of small mites, allergens, and even bacteria and viruses that get trapped in the particles. Reducing the amount of dust in your aair helps you to breathe easier.

You may not get sick as often thanks to fewer particles cycling through the system and back into your air. Plus, You can reduce allergy symptoms if you are susceptible to having allergies at any time of the year. Breathing easier can also mean that you sleep better, which is another way to improve your health. When you sleep better, your body is better able to fight off illnesses and allergies when you do experience them.

Improve HVAC Operation

Reducing the amount of dust circulating through your home also helps your HVAC system. The same particles getting in your ductwork are also interfering with HVAC operation by creating grime in between intricate gears and bearings. 

Instead of HVAC system that is able to work efficiently, yours will be working harder against a grime buildup thanks to dust. When you stay on top of regular duct cleanings, you can greatly improve how your HVAC system operates, especially toward the end of its lifespan. This is because you are reducing the amount of strain it takes on.

item.title: Signs It’s Time for a New Heater Installation
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
A reliable heating system is essential for keeping your home comfortable as temperatures drop. But how do you know when it’s time to replace your old heater?

Sometimes, a repair isn’t enough, and installing a new system becomes the best option. Here are the key signs that it’s time for a new heater installation.

1. Age of the Heater
The lifespan of most heating systems is between 15 to 20 years. If your heater is nearing or surpassing this age, it’s likely time to consider a replacement. An older heater not only becomes less efficient but may also require frequent repairs, making it a costly option in the long run.

2. Rising Energy Bills
Have you noticed a steady increase in your energy bills, even though your heating habits haven’t changed? This could indicate that your heater is losing efficiency and working harder to heat your home. Newer models are designed with energy-saving technology, which can significantly reduce your monthly utility costs.

3. Frequent Repairs
If you’re calling a technician every few months, it might be time to invest in a new system. Constant repairs can quickly add up, often approaching or exceeding the cost of a new heater. A one-time investment in a modern heating system can save you time, money, and stress.

4. Inconsistent Temperatures
Does your home feel unevenly heated? Are some rooms too hot while others remain chilly? This could be a sign that your current heater is struggling to distribute heat evenly. A new heater can resolve this issue, providing consistent warmth throughout your home.

5. Strange Noises
Loud banging, rattling, or squealing noises coming from your heater are not normal. These sounds often point to serious mechanical problems that may not be worth repairing. Upgrading to a new heater eliminates these noises and ensures quiet, efficient operation.

6. Poor Air Quality
An outdated heater may circulate dust, allergens, and other particles, reducing your home’s air quality. If you notice an increase in allergies, respiratory issues, or dry air, your heater might be the culprit. Newer systems often include advanced filtration and humidification features to improve indoor air quality.

7. Safety Concerns
Older heaters can pose safety risks, such as carbon monoxide leaks or electrical hazards. If your heater has a cracked heat exchanger or other serious issues, it’s critical to replace it immediately. Prioritize your family’s safety with a modern, dependable heating system.

Partner With Experts for Your Heater Installation
When the time comes to replace your heating system, choosing the right professionals makes all the difference. If you’re in search of a heater installation in Gainesville, GA, and need a dependable team, look no further than American Comfort for expert installation and reliable service. Their experienced technicians ensure your new system is installed efficiently and correctly, giving you peace of mind during the colder months.

Investing in a new heater is not just about comfort—it’s about improving your home’s energy efficiency, safety, and overall quality of life. Don’t wait until your heater fails; act before the winter chill takes hold.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heater installation or repairs in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Sometimes, a repair isn’t enough, and installing a new system becomes the best option.

item.title: Tips for Mindful Heater Use
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
If you’re looking for ways to boost heater efficiency and save money at the same time, you’re in the right place. Our team of experts has compiled a list of tips to help you make the most of your heater in the coming winter season without spending more on your monthly energy costs. 

If you ever need heater service, maintenance, or even heater installation service in Gainesville, GA, give our team a call to schedule an appointment. We can help you with all of your heating needs. Keep reading to learn more about how to stay warm this winter season without breaking the bank.

Thermostat Settings

First, you have to consider the temperature setting you use on your thermostat. If you set the temperature too high in an attempt to warm up your home, you’ll just leave your heater working harder to produce impossible results. This is especially true as temperatures dip lower and lower in the peak of the winter season. 

Experts estimate that you should not set your thermostat higher than 68° for heating in the winter. Any higher than that and you’re adding unnecessary strain to your heater. Plus, if your heater consumes more energy, your gas and electric bills can skyrocket.

Air Vent Usage

You also want to monitor all of your air vents to ensure each one is open completely and not blocked by any type of furniture. We find that many homeowners close air vents in one room in an attempt to channel more heat to another room. Unfortunately, this doesn’t really work. Instead, heat just gets trapped in your ductwork because it has nowhere to go in the areas where vents are closed. 

If you’re having issues with one room not getting as warm as the others, just give our team a call and schedule an appointment for us to check out your ductwork and make adjustments. We can help you ensure maximum energy efficiency and airflow through your entire home. 

If your air vents are on the floor, you want to make sure that no furniture is placed on top, potentially blocking heat from entering your home. If your air vents are located on the ceiling, the same thing goes but in relation to taller furniture like bookshelves.

Bundle Up

Setting the thermostat temperature down lower can be an adjustment if you’re used to keeping your home warmer. Plan to wear a sweater, socks, and even a hat. Bundle up with blankets and consider investing in an electric blanket, too. You’ll still be warm, but without spending as much on your monthly energy costs.

Plus, by reducing strain on your heater you’re also saving money on potential repair costs and even by extending the expected lifespan of the system. If adjusting the thermostat down all at once is too much for you, try adjusting it a single degree at a time until you feel more comfortable with the lower setting.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heater maintenance or repairs in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn more about how to stay warm this winter season without breaking the bank.

item.title: AC Maintenance Tips for the End of the Season
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Summer is long gone and we are well into the fall season. But before you say goodbye to air conditioning for good this year, you need to take some steps to maintain your air conditioner so it is ready for next spring.

Aside from the steps that you take on your own, you can also invest in professional HVAC repair in Winder, GA. Keep reading to learn our tips for maintaining your air conditioner ahead of the winter season. Then give our team a call to schedule your maintenance or repair appointment.

Place a New Air Filter

One easy thing that you can do for your air conditioner is to you swap out the existing air filter for a new one. Even though your air conditioner will sit largely unused over the winter season, when spring rolls around you’ll want your AC to be ready to go. Having a clean air filter in place means that you can turn on your air conditioner without taking extra steps to do any maintenance then. You’ll already have a clean air filter in place and ready to go.

Clean the Condensate Drain Line

You also need to rinse out the condensate drain line with vinegar. Otherwise, you run the risk of mold developing inside the line thanks to moisture that is left behind. This is a step you should be completing for your air conditioner on a monthly basis anyway, but it is even more important to complete it before turning your air conditioner off completely.

Otherwise, a clog can form due to the mold growth. Then, when you turn your air conditioner on next spring, you may find that water is leaking out and you need to schedule a professional service. This proactive step will go a long way toward helping your AC work at its best and improve indoor air quality.

Check the Outdoor Unit

It’s actually a good idea to check the outdoor unit throughout the winter season. Even if it is not operational, it is still taking on dirt and debris simply by being outside. Go ahead and rinse your AC off with your water hose and remove any trash, leaves, or sticks that may have blown into your air conditioner. Then check it periodically throughout the winter season to stay ahead of any potential problems.

Check the Indoor Unit

If you want to go a step further, you can also open the cabinet of your indoor unit and gently wipe it out. We recommend that you be very careful to not near fear with any of the connections. But you can wipe off the portions of the cabinet itself, as well as the coils inside. Dust can build up as time passes and keeping the inside of the cabinet clean is a great way to boost AC efficiency for next summer.

Pay Attention to Signs of Problems

As you complete these steps for your AC, be sure to pay attention to the signs that something is wrong with your AC. If you notice any loose connections or components that look broken, give us a call to check it out. It’s better to be safe and have our team look at your AC than to risk greater problems later on.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater repair in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn our tips for maintaining your air conditioner ahead of the winter season.

item.title: Do Tankless Water Heaters Need More Repairs?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When considering if a more expensive version of something is financially worth it, it’s important to consider not just the initial cost, but the potential expenses of owning it. For example, perhaps you can afford an expensive car, but you also need to consider how much insurance, gas, maintenance, upkeep, and repairs will cost.

When deciding between a tankless or a tank water heater, it’s a good idea to think about the long-term costs. Although tankless water heaters will give the benefit of reduced utility costs, are there other financial considerations to know about? Let’s go over whether tankless water heaters need expensive water heater repair in Lawrenceville, GA. 

How Tankless Water Heaters Work 

A traditional tank water heater stores and heats water 24/7, ensuring hot water is available 24/7. As you can imagine, this requires a substantial amount of energy. But tankless water heaters do not have a tank (hence the name) so you don’t have to worry about a tank corroding. They heat water on demand (which is why they’re also called on-demand water heaters) which results in lower utility costs.

Tankless water heaters are coveted for their energy efficiency and long lifespans, but they are still prone to the occasional repair need. Some common repair needs for tankless water heaters include:

  • Mineral buildup: Hard water in our area can lead to mineral and sediment buildup, reducing its efficiency and eventually causing the system to malfunction. However, we can regularly flush the system to combat the negative effects of hard water.
  • Sensor malfunctions: Tankless systems rely on sensors to detect water flow and signal the heating element to start. These sensors can malfunction or become blocked, resulting in inconsistent water temperatures or a lack of hot water.
  • Ignition problems: Gas-powered tankless water heaters have ignition systems that can fail over time, resulting in the system’s inability to heat water properly. 
  • Electrical component failures: For electric tankless systems, electrical components can sometimes fail due to wear and tear over time, requiring repairs or replacements.

Lifespan 

Tankless water heaters are known for their durability, often lasting up to 20 years with proper maintenance. But tank water heaters usually only last for 10-15 years because heating and storing water 24/7 incurs tremendous wear and tear. 

Because tankless systems last longer and incur less wear and tear, they may need fewer repairs. This increased lifespan also contributes to tankless water heaters being more cost-effective over the long run when you need to replace your system less frequently.

Both Types Require Maintenance 

No matter what system you choose, both tankless and tank water heaters will need regular maintenance. Because you live in an area with hard water, flushing a tankless system to remove mineral buildup can help prevent unexpected repairs. Routine inspections and maintenance are the best ways to minimize repair needs for both systems.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater repair in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: When deciding between a tankless or a tank water heater, it’s a good idea to think about the long-term costs including if they need more repairs.

item.title: Should I Repair or Replace My Water Heater?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
A water heater is a major home comfort system that you can’t afford to be without for even a day. Although a water heater isn’t as expensive to replace as an air conditioner or heating system, they’re still pricey. 

As a homeowner, it’s helpful to know when an ailing water heater can get by with a repair and when it’s time to put that money into a water heater replacement in Gainesville, GA. Let’s go over when a water heater should be repaired vs replaced so that you can make an informed decision when the time comes.

When to Repair a Water Heater

Here are some scenarios when a water heater should be repaired.

  • Minor leaks: A small leak from a valve or fitting might be easily fixed without replacing the entire unit. A professional plumber can often replace a faulty part and have your water heater running smoothly again.
  • The repair is inexpensive: If the repair estimate isn’t a lot, it could be worth paying for an inexpensive repair.
  • It’s under its lifespan: A tank water heater can last 8-12 years and a tankless water heater can last 15 years. If your water heater is well under these lifespans, it should be repaired.

When to Replace a Water Heater

Here are some scenarios when a water heater should be replaced

  • Frequent repair needs: If you find yourself constantly calling for repairs, the costs can quickly add up. Frequent repairs are a sign that the unit is nearing the end of its lifespan.
  • Rust-colored water: When you notice rust in your hot water, this can mean that the tank is corroding. Corrosion inside the water heater can’t be fixed and should be replaced ASAP to avoid a major leak.
  • It’s within its lifespan: Unless the repair is inexpensive, most of the time, it’s not worth paying for a repair on an old and outdated system.

Signs Your Water Heater Needs Help

Here are some signs that your water heater is struggling to keep up with the demands of your household.

  • Lack of hot water: If your water heater can’t meet the demands of your household and is unable to supply enough hot water for basic things such as showering, schedule an appointment with us to see if a repair is needed.
  • Strange noises: Unusual sounds like rumbling, popping, or banging can indicate a buildup of sediment from hard water or a failing heating element.
  • Leaks: Your water heater should not leak. If you notice water around the base, leaks from the tank, dripping from the valves, or any other unusual water, schedule an appointment with us for a prompt repair.
  • Discolored water: Rust-colored or cloudy water often indicates that the interior of the water heater tank is corroding. Don’t ignore these water heater issues if you want to avoid more serious damage or unexpected breakdowns.

Are you still not sure if your water heater can be salvaged or if it needs a full replacement? Discuss your best option with our team to ensure your comfort and efficiency.

Frequently Asked Questions

Question: When is it better to repair my water heater?
Answer: Repairing is often the best choice when the issue is minor, such as a faulty thermostat, heating element, or valve. If your water heater is still under 8 to 10 years old and the repair costs are low, fixing it can be a more cost-effective option than replacing it.

Question: What are signs that my water heater needs replacement instead of a repair?
Answer: Replacement is usually the better choice if the unit has frequent breakdowns, is over 10 years old, shows rust in the water, produces insufficient hot water, or has major leaks. These signs suggest the water heater is nearing the end of its lifespan.

Question: How does the age of the unit affect the repair vs replacement decision?
Answer: The age of the water heater plays a major role. Tank models typically last 8 to 12 years, while tankless models can last 15 to 20 years. Once a unit reaches the end of its expected lifespan, replacement is usually more cost-effective than repeated repairs.

Question: Is it always better to replace if repair costs are high?
Answer: A common guideline is the 50% rule: if a repair costs more than half the price of a new water heater, replacement is usually the smarter investment. This helps avoid ongoing repair expenses and ensures better reliability and efficiency.

Question: Can proper maintenance extend the life of my water heater?
Answer: Yes. Regular maintenance like flushing the tank, inspecting the anode rod, and checking for sediment buildup can extend the unit’s lifespan. Simple upkeep prevents major issues, helps maintain efficiency, and can delay the need for replacement.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater replacement in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: As a homeowner, it’s helpful to know when an ailing water heater can get by with a repair and when it’s time to put that money into replacement.

item.title: Drawbacks of Portable Air Quality Devices
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Portable air quality devices have become popular among homeowners who want to improve their indoor air quality (IAQ). These cheap and compact units promise to reduce allergens, pollutants, humidity, and odors, making the air in your home cleaner and healthier. 

However, portable air quality devices have many drawbacks that can limit their effectiveness and value. Let’s go over the shortcomings of portable air filters and air purifiers and why you’re better off with a whole house solution. We provide indoor air quality services in Winder, GA that truly solve the issue of poor IAQ.

Limited Coverage Area

The biggest drawback of portable air quality devices is their limited coverage area. They are designed to work in small spaces such as a single bedroom or office. If you’re trying to improve air quality in a larger room or an entire home, you’ll need multiple units which can get expensive especially when you consider the ongoing cost of replacement filters for each unit.

Noise Levels

A common issue with portable air purifiers is noise. They produce noticeable sound, especially when operating at higher settings. Even “whisper-quiet” systems will still make discernible noise, disrupting your home’s quiet environments such as bedrooms and home offices. The noise generated can be particularly annoying at night when you’re trying to sleep. 

Filter Replacement Costs

Portable devices require regular filter changes. Depending on the model, replacement filters can cost anywhere from $20 to over $100 each, and they need to be replaced frequently. The cumulative costs of filters and maintenance can make portable air purifiers an expensive solution, especially when compared to whole-home systems that integrate with your HVAC system.

Inefficiency in Addressing Certain Pollutants

Portable air quality devices are often seen as comprehensive solutions, but they have their limits when it comes to eliminating certain pollutants. Many portable air purifiers are not effective at removing larger particles, gases, or volatile organic compounds (VOCs). 

Homeowners may assume that having an air purifier running means their indoor air quality issues are solved when the device is only making a small dent in your home’s IAQ. Meanwhile, they’re left wondering why allergy symptoms are still bothersome and their home’s air still seems stuffy and smelly.

Why Whole-House Solutions Are Best

Whole house air purifiers and dehumidifiers are installed by professional HVAC technicians like those on our team. They are integrated into your existing HVAC system and when properly installed, will purify, clean, and dehumidify all the air that circulates throughout your entire home. 

You’ll no longer have to worry about whether you have enough portable devices to tackle all the rooms of your home. Not only can we install a whole-house solution for you, but we can also recommend systems based on your specific concerns and needs. You can rest assured that you have an economical and stress-free solution to your home’s poor indoor air quality problems. 

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for indoor air quality services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Portable air quality devices have many drawbacks that can limit their effectiveness and value. Let’s go over their shortcomings and why a whole house solution is better.

item.title: New Air Conditioning Technologies to Know About
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
There are few areas in the US that depend on air conditioning systems as much as you do in The Peach State. With hot, sticky, humid weather that persists throughout an extended cooling season, a strong and powerful air conditioning system is your ally to beat the heat. 

Air conditioning systems have advanced rapidly over the last decade or so. With a focus on energy efficiency, today’s central air conditioners and heat pumps are designed to maximize your home comfort while minimizing your utility costs. If you’re suffering because of an outdated system, it could be time for an upgrade. Let’s go over the new AC technologies you can look forward to in a new system.

Inverter Technology

One of the most significant advancements in AC technology is inverter technology. Traditional air conditioners operate in either two modes: on or off. When they’re on, they run at full capacity until the desired temperature is reached. 

Inverter technology allows the AC system to run at multiple speeds. That means it can run at a low setting when it’s not as hot outside or when your home doesn’t need full-speed cooling power. An inverter system knows then to adjust the compressor speed to maximize its energy efficiency. You really can’t go wrong with an inverter air conditioner in Lawrenceville, GA. 

  • Improved energy efficiency: By adjusting the compressor speed, inverter ACs consume less electricity compared to traditional or standard units.
  • Quieter operation: Inverter ACs run more smoothly and quietly, making them ideal for homeowners who don’t like to hear their AC system running.
  • Precise temperature control: The ability to fine-tune the compressor speed provides accurate temperature regulation, resulting in fewer utility costs.

Heat Pumps

Heat pumps have become increasingly popular in climates like ours in Georgia with mild winter temperatures and an extended cooling season. Unlike traditional air conditioners that only provide cooling, heat pumps can reverse their operation to provide heating as well. This dual functionality makes them a cost-effective solution for year-round climate control. Heat pumps come in ducted and ductless systems.

  1. Ducted heat pumps: These systems transfer heat between the indoor air and the outside air. They are highly efficient and can provide substantial energy savings, especially in moderate climates. They have a similar setup to central air conditioners with an outdoor and indoor unit.
  2. Ductless mini-split heat pumps: These systems are ideal for homes without existing ductwork or for those looking to add AC to specific rooms. They consist of an outdoor unit connected to one or more indoor units, providing targeted heating and cooling and amazing zone control. 

The benefits of heat pumps include:

  • Inverter technology: Some heat pumps are equipped with the same inverter technology available in central air conditioners.
  • Energy efficiency: Heat pumps use less electricity compared to traditional heating systems, leading to lower utility bills.
  • Versatility: The ability to provide both heating and cooling makes heat pumps a convenient and space-saving solution.

Are you ready to discover how advancements in AC technology can boost your home comfort? Contact us today to learn more!

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air conditioning services in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: With a focus on energy efficiency, today’s central air conditioners and heat pumps are designed to maximize your home comfort while minimizing your utility costs.

item.title: What Are the Signs of an AC Refrigerant Leak?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
One of the most common issues with central air conditioners and heat pumps is a refrigerant leak. Although these systems are designed to hold the full amount of refrigerant they should ever need across their 10-15-year lifespan, leaks can develop, leading to an insufficient amount of refrigerant for the AC to work properly.

Leaks can develop for a variety of reasons: an amateur or DIY installation job, faulty equipment, factory defects, wear and tear over time, and corrosion. Not only is breathing in refrigerant dangerous, but your AC system will never be able to cool down your home efficiently if it is leaking refrigerant. Let’s go over six signs that your AC has a refrigerant leak that will require air conditioning repair in Winder, GA.

1. Decreased Cooling Output

One of the most obvious signs of a leaky air conditioner is decreased cooling output. This can manifest as either weak airflow or warm airflow. You will notice that your home is not reaching the set temperature on the thermostat. It’ll also seem like the AC system is running a lot but failing to do its job.

2. Increased Energy Bills

This is one of the least obvious signs of a refrigerant leak since it requires paying attention to your monthly utility bills. If you notice a spike in costs that can’t be correlated to increased usage, a refrigerant leak could be to blame, but you will usually notice some of these other symptoms.

3. Hissing or Bubbling Sounds

Often, refrigerant leaks are caused by pinhole leaks somewhere in the system. Depending on where the leak is, you may hear hissing or bubbling sounds. Refrigerant in an AC system is maintained at high pressure. When there is a leak, the pressurized gas escapes through the small gap or hole, leading to a hissing sound. Because refrigerant is dangerous to breathe in, hissing and bubbling noises should be addressed quickly by a professional HVAC company.

4. Ice Formation on Evaporator Coils

Ice formation on the evaporator coils occurs when there is a significant drop in pressure. When the refrigerant level is insufficient, it cannot absorb the necessary amount of heat from the indoor air and the evaporator coils become excessively cold. This extreme cold causes the moisture in the surrounding air to freeze upon contact with the coils, leading to ice formation. 

5. AC System Running Constantly

If you notice that your AC system seems to be running constantly without decreasing your home’s temperature, this is almost certainly a sign of a refrigerant leak. As the system struggles to perform without enough refrigerant, it’ll keep running in an attempt to reach the desired temperature on the thermostat.

6. High Humidity Levels

Have you noticed that your home seems more humid than usual? Your air conditioner naturally reduces humidity when it is functioning properly. If your humidity levels are through the roof, chances are the AC system isn’t working properly.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air conditioning repair services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Let’s go over six signs that your AC has a refrigerant leak that will require air conditioning repair in Winder, GA.

item.title: What Is the Most Economical Air Conditioning System
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to cooling your home efficiently and affordably, selecting the right air conditioning system is crucial. With various options available, it can be challenging to determine which system offers the best balance of initial cost and long-term savings. 

In this blog post, we’ll explore central air conditioning systems, mini split systems, and heat pumps, focusing on their costs and benefits to help you identify the most economical choice for air conditioning replacement in Gainesville, GA.

Central Air Conditioning Systems

Central air conditioning systems are a popular choice for many homeowners due to their ability to cool an entire house efficiently. These systems use a network of ducts to distribute cool air throughout the home.

Modern central AC systems are relatively energy-efficient, especially those with high SEER2 (Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio 2) ratings. However, the operational costs can be significant due to the energy required to cool an entire home.
