Mini Split Systems

Mini split systems, also known as ductless air conditioners, offer a flexible and energy-efficient alternative to central AC. These systems consist of an outdoor compressor and one or more indoor units that can be mounted in different rooms.

The initial cost of mini split systems can be lower than central AC systems. Installation is simpler since it doesn’t require ductwork, making it less invasive and quicker.

Mini splits are highly energy-efficient due to their ability to cool specific areas or rooms individually. This targeted cooling can lead to significant energy savings, especially in homes where certain areas are used more frequently than others.

Heat Pump Systems

Heat pumps are versatile systems that can provide both cooling and heating, making them an excellent year-round solution. They work by transferring heat from one place to another, using electricity efficiently.

Heat pumps generally have a higher initial cost compared to central AC systems but offer dual functionality. Upfront costs can vary depending on its energy efficiency with more efficient models costing more.

Heat pumps are known for their superior energy efficiency, particularly in climates with hot summers and mild winters. They use less electricity compared to traditional heating and cooling systems, resulting in lower operational costs over time.

Long-term Cost Savings

When evaluating the most economical air conditioning system, it’s essential to consider long-term cost savings in addition to initial expenses. Both mini splits and heat pumps tend to offer greater long-term savings compared to central AC systems.

Homes with variable occupancy or rooms that are not used frequently can benefit significantly from the zone cooling capabilities of mini splits. Small to medium-sized homes or homes with specific cooling needs can achieve substantial savings with mini split systems by avoiding the energy loss associated with ductwork.

Investing in the right air conditioning system not only ensures comfort but also optimizes your energy use, leading to significant savings over time. Whether you opt for the all-encompassing central AC, the flexible mini split, or the efficient heat pump, understanding the benefits and costs of each option will guide you toward the most economical choice for your home.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air conditioning repair services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Let's go over central air conditioning systems, mini split systems, and heat pumps, focusing on their costs and benefits to help you identify the most economical choice.

item.title: Which Type of Air Conditioner Is the Quietest?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to creating a comfortable home environment, the noise level of your air conditioner is a crucial factor to consider. Nobody wants to be disturbed by a loud AC system while trying to relax or sleep. Understanding the different types of air conditioners and their associated noise levels can help you make an informed decision for a quieter home. 

In this post, we’ll explore the sound levels of central air conditioners and heat pumps, along with other options, to help you find the quietest system for your needs. Be sure to contact us for air conditioning service in Lawrenceville, GA

Air Conditioner Noise Levels

Air conditioner noise levels are typically measured in decibels (dB). For context, whispering measures around 20 dB, while normal conversation ranges from 50 to 60 dB. Residential air conditioners usually fall between 40 and 70 dB. Keep in mind that the listed dB levels are when AC systems are operating at their quietest.

Several factors contribute to the noise levels of an air conditioner, including the type of compressor, the speed of the fan, and the quality of installation. High-efficiency models with variable-speed compressors and well-designed fan blades tend to operate more quietly. Additionally, proper installation and regular maintenance can significantly reduce noise.

Central Air Conditioners

The noise levels of central air conditioners can vary, but most modern units are designed to operate quietly. High-efficiency models often feature variable speed compressors and sound-dampening technology that keep noise levels low. 

On average, a well-maintained central air conditioner will produce noise levels between 55 and 70 dB. Opting for units with a noise level closer to 55 dB can ensure a quieter home environment.

Heat Pumps

In terms of noise levels, heat pumps are comparable to central air conditioners. Most modern heat pumps are designed with noise reduction in mind, featuring insulated compressors and variable-speed fans. 

The average noise level for a heat pump ranges from 50 to 65 dB. Dual-stage compressors, which operate at lower speeds for most of the time, can further reduce noise, making heat pumps a quiet and efficient option for many homeowners.

Ductless Mini-Split Systems 

Ductless mini-splits are known for their quiet operation, often producing noise levels as low as 20 to 30 dB for indoor units and 50 to 60 dB for outdoor units.

Tips for Choosing a Quiet Air Conditioner

When searching for a quiet air conditioner, consider the following tips:

  • Check the Decibel Rating: Look for the manufacturer’s specifications on noise levels. Units with a lower dB rating will operate more quietly.
  • Brand Reputation: Ask us which brands are known for producing quiet air conditioners. 
  • Professional Installation: Ensure your air conditioner is installed correctly by a professional HVAC company. Poor installation can lead to increased noise levels.
  • Regular Maintenance: Keep your air conditioner running smoothly with regular maintenance scheduled once a year for central AC and twice a year for heat pumps.

If you’re looking for the quietest air conditioning options, we’re here to help you find the perfect solution for a comfortable and quiet home.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air conditioning services in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Let's explore the sound levels of central air conditioners and heat pumps, along with other options, to help you find the quietest system for your needs.

item.title: Does My AC Help With High Humidity?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When you’re in need of AC repair in Gainesville, GA, one of the first things you’ll notice is high humidity in your home. High humidity levels can be incredibly uncomfortable, leading to sticky, oppressive air, and can even cause damage to your home and health issues. 

Many people wonder if their air conditioner can help reduce this humidity. The good news is that air conditioners do play a significant role in reducing humidity levels. However, they are not the only solution. Let’s explore how air conditioners help with humidity and look at other effective methods for keeping your home’s humidity in check.

How Air Conditioners Reduce Humidity

Air conditioners are designed to cool your home, but they also remove moisture from the air in the process. When warm air from your home is pulled into the AC unit, it passes over the evaporator coil. This coil contains a cold refrigerant that cools the air, causing moisture in the air to condense on the coil, much like water droplets forming on a cold glass of water on a hot day. This condensed moisture is then drained away, leaving the air in your home cooler and drier.

Benefits of Air Conditioning for Humidity Control

Using an air conditioner to control humidity has several benefits:

  • Improved Comfort Levels: Lower humidity levels make the air feel cooler and more comfortable, reducing the need for very low thermostat settings.
  • Prevention of Mold and Mildew Growth: High humidity creates an ideal environment for mold and mildew, which can damage your home and pose health risks. An AC helps keep these at bay.
  • Protection of Furniture and Infrastructure: Excess moisture can warp wood, peel paint, and cause other damage to your home’s interior. Reducing humidity helps protect your investment.

Other Methods to Control Humidity at Home

While air conditioners are effective, they are not the only tools available for controlling humidity. Here are some other methods to consider:

Whole House Dehumidifiers

Dehumidifiers are devices specifically designed to remove moisture from the air. They work similarly to air conditioners but without the cooling effect. Air is drawn into the dehumidifier, where it passes over cold coils to condense moisture. The dry air is then released back into the room. Getting a whole house dehumidifier installed to work with an AC system can be particularly effective in very humid climates like ours here in Georgia.

Regular Maintenance

Keeping your AC unit in good condition is essential. Regularly clean and replace filters to ensure proper airflow and efficient operation. Schedule maintenance on a regular basis for your central air conditioning, central heat pump, or ductless mini split system. When your AC system is running at peak performance, you shouldn’t have issues with excessive humidity.

Air conditioners are a powerful tool for reducing humidity in your home, enhancing comfort, and protecting your property. However, they work best when combined with other humidity control methods like a whole house dehumidifier. Contact us to learn more about how you can achieve balanced humidity in your home.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for AC repair services in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Let's explore how air conditioners help with humidity and look at other effective methods for keeping your home's humidity in check.

item.title: Is Air Duct Cleaning a Scam?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Many homeowners want to know if air duct cleaning is really something that they need. Well, we’d say look at it this way: what would happen if you never cleaned your home? Imagine the layers of dust and dirt that would quickly develop!

Although your home’s air ducts are tucked behind walls or in basements, they’re also a delivery system for the conditioned air that your HVAC system generates. As such, the same dust, dirt, and dander that would accumulate throughout your home can also be found in your air ducts. 

Just as your home would accumulate grime, mold, dust, and dander if you never cleaned, your air ducts accumulate the same things. That’s why air duct cleaning in Winder, GA is a recommended service that should be performed every 3-5 years. Let’s go over the many benefits of this necessary service.

The “V” in HVAC: Ventilation

As we alluded to earlier, it’s essential to understand the role of air ducts in HVAC systems. Your home’s ventilation system–its ductwork or air ducts–is the pathway through which conditioned air moves from the HVAC unit to your home’s living spaces. Over time, these ducts can accumulate pet and human dander, dust, dust mites, mold, and other contaminants.

The Role of Air Duct Cleaning

The accumulation of contaminants in ductwork can have negative effects on both indoor air quality and how your HVAC system operates. Contaminants that are trapped in the ductwork will circulate throughout the home when the HVAC system is running, leading to the need to dust more often, reduced indoor air quality, and increased allergy symptoms.

Dirty ductwork hinders your HVAC system’s airflow, causing it to work harder to achieve the set temperature. It’s been estimated that a layer no more than 1/16 of an inch can reduce your blower efficiency by 20 percent. Any extra wear and tear that your HVAC system accumulates can be detrimental, even if it’s a minor amount.

Professional Vs. DIY Duct Cleaning

So, now you’re convinced that there is merit to the concept of air duct cleaning and that it’s not a scam. But perhaps you’re thinking this is something that you can do yourself. There are many reasons why we wouldn’t recommend a DIY attempt at air duct cleaning. 

First of all, your ductwork is a large network of delicate sheet metalwork. Not only would it be impossible to reach this entire network of ducts without the use of professional tools, but you also run the risk of damaging your ductwork.

For a job of this magnitude, it’s best to leave it to professionals like those on our team. Not only do we have the tools and techniques down, but we also have the skills and experience to do the job thoroughly. And when it’s done right the first time, you can rest assured that you won’t need this service again for another 3-5 years.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air duct cleaning services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Your home would accumulate dirt, dust, and dander if you never cleaned, and these also accumulate in air ducts. Air duct cleaning is needed every 3-5 years.

item.title: Central AC, Heat Pump, or Mini Split Installation?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When it’s time to replace or install an air conditioning system, you’ve got a big decision to make. Not only do you need to choose which type of system to get, but once you narrow that down, you need to decide on a make and model. We understand how important these decisions are which is why we want you to know what your options are.

Installation day is the most important day in the life of an AC system. So no matter what system you choose, let us be the HVAC company that installs it for you. Our NATE-certified technicians embrace our motto of “Bringing comfort to America, one home at a time.” That means we focus on our customers for every HVAC Service in Lawrenceville, GA we provide. 

Central Air Conditioning

Central air conditioners remain the most popular system across the US. In Georgia, homeowners often choose central AC because these systems can quickly and powerfully cool down a home. 

The efficiency of a central AC system is measured by its SEER2 rating. Here in Georgia, the minimum rating for our region is 14.3. However, SEER2 ratings go much higher. Although a system with a higher SEER2 rating will cost more, you’ll save money on utility costs for as long as you own the system. The lifespan of central AC units is 10-15 years.

Not only will we install your new air conditioner perfectly, we can also help fit you with the right type of air conditioner. We can recommend the top-of-the-line brands within your budget. Next, we’ll assess your home to make sure the system is sized correctly. We can even evaluate your ductwork to make sure it’ll be a great support system for your new central AC. After all, your system relies on ductwork to deliver the conditioned air throughout your home.

Heat Pumps

Heat pumps are a great option for homeowners who are looking for an all-in-one HVAC system. Heat pumps rely on refrigerant to provide heating and cooling. A component called the reversing valve allows a heat pump to easily switch between heating and cooling modes. 

Heat pumps use electricity and are considered extremely energy efficient compared to central AC. That’s because they simply transfer heat instead of generating heat. Just like with central AC systems, heat pump efficiency is measured by SEER2 for cooling.

Ductless Mini Splits

Ductless mini splits are also heat pumps. However, the only difference between the two systems is that they don’t require ductwork. When central AC isn’t right for your home, a ductless system is a great alternative. 

Ductless mini splits can solve a number of issues for homeowners with older homes with problematic ductwork that would render a ducted central AC or heat pump ineffective. Because they don’t use ductwork, they’re ideal for homeowners who are concerned about poor indoor air quality.

Ductless systems also provide amazing customization with the ability to change the temperature in a single zone or room in your house, adding to their already low energy consumption. 

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for HVAC service in Lawrenceville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: We understand how important choosing a new air conditioning system for your Georgia home is. That's why we want you to know what your options are.

item.title: What&#8217;s a Good SEER2 Rating for My AC?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When you are due for AC Installation in Gainesville, GA, there are a lot of factors to take into consideration when choosing your new AC system. In addition to choosing what type of system you will have installed, you need to consider the system’s efficiency rating and your budget.

Although some homeowners may be tempted to go with the least expensive model available, it pays to spend a little more on a system with a higher efficiency rating. That’s because you will hopefully own this system for the next 10 to 15 years and any reduction in your utility expenses will help. Let’s go over the most important factors you should consider.

Central AC and Heat Pumps: SEER2 Ratings

If you are considering getting a central air conditioning system, a ducted heat pump, or a ductless heat pump (also known as a mini split system), one of the first things you should consider is its SEER2 rating. The SEER2 indicates how efficient the system is when it’s in cooling mode. 

The SEER2 rating Is the biggest determining factor in how much your new system will cost. That’s because the higher the rating, the more energy-efficient the system is. In order for an AC system to be energy efficient, it must have technologically advanced features, and these features often come at a higher price.

For every state in the US, there is a minimum SEER2 rating based on geographic area. Georgia is in the southern region and the minimum SEER2 rating is 14.3 for both heat pumps and central AC systems. Central air conditioners and heat pumps have SEER2 ratings as high as 18. Although you may initially have doubts over the expense of a highly efficient system, you have to remember that it will cost you less in the long run. 

Enjoy Better Indoor Comfort With a High SEER2 Rating

Choosing an air conditioning unit with a higher SEER2 rating provides enhanced home comfort during the summer months which is particularly beneficial in hot and humid regions like ours with an extended cooling season. These systems tend to operate more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of encountering uncontrollable humidity, high utility bills, or AC issues.

Advanced SEER2 units typically incorporate two key components to optimize indoor comfort:

  • Two-stage or variable-speed compressors
  • Variable-speed blowers

In contrast, air conditioners with lower SEER2 ratings are often single-stage, operating solely at one speed. Standard AC and heat pump systems can struggle to adequately control humidity levels over time which prompts homeowners to dial down the thermostat. You’ll need to consider if the system will be able to adequately combat our high humidity levels.

Are you still uncertain what SEER2 rating is best for your new AC system? We can advise you, taking into consideration your household makeup, your long-term goals for your home, and your budget.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for AC installation services in Gainesville, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: One of the first things you should consider when choosing a new AC is its SEER2 rating which indicates how efficient the system is.

item.title: Get Ahead of Georgia&#8217;s Allergy Season
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Georgia is home to many types of trees, plants, and vegetation that contribute to our beautiful and iconic scenery. In fact, Georgia has 66% forest coverage and is ranked 7th on the list of states with the most forest. Ask any Georgia resident and they’ll agree that Georgia is one of the most beautiful states in the country.

People spend a significant amount of their time indoors, making indoor air quality especially important.

But unfortunately, all those trees come at a price. Many people suffer from allergy symptoms due to the abundance of tree pollen. With the arrival of spring, you and your family members may be suffering from allergy symptoms. 

Although there’s not much you can do to avoid sneezing and water eyes when you go outside, there are steps you can take in your home to combat their effects. Keep reading to discover how our air purifier services in Winder, GA can help you combat allergies not just in spring, but all year long.

Many factors contribute to indoor air quality inside the home, not just outdoor pollen.

Human activities such as cooking and cleaning can also affect indoor air quality and allergy symptoms.

Common Allergens in Georgia’s Spring Air

In Georgia, allergy season usually begins as early as February as tree pollen levels sharply increase. Here are some of the most common allergens in Georgia based on the season. As you can see, there’s really no escaping allergy symptoms throughout the year.

  • Spring: Major triggers for allergies are from tree pollen from species such as oak, hickory, walnut, maple, ash, pine, and cedar.
  • Summer: Summer allergy season starts as early as May and peaks in June. Grasses such as Bermuda grass, Kentucky Bluegrass, Timothy grass, and Rye Grass release their pollen. Also, the heat and humidity allow mold spores to flourish.
  • Fall: Fall brings little relief to allergy sufferers. Here are fall’s common allergens: Ragweed, Cocklebur, Burning bush, Lamb’s quarter, Pigweed, Mugwort, Russian thistle, Tumbleweed, Sagebrush
  • Winter: Winter allergies are usually due to indoor allergens such as dust mites, pet dander, and mold spores. These indoor allergens can affect indoor air quality and lead to health issues.

Exposure to these seasonal allergens is associated with health risks, including respiratory problems and other health effects.

How Airborne Allergens Enter Your Georgia Home

Here’s how they make their way into your home in the first place.

  • Open doors and windows: Letting in fresh spring air after being cooped up all winter allows allergens to drift inside.
  • HVAC system: Allergens can infiltrate your HVAC system via air intake vents, open doors and windows, and holes and leaks in ductwork. Air flow within the home can carry these allergens to different rooms, so managing air flow is important for controlling indoor air quality. 
  • Pets and people: As pets and family members go outside and return home, they bring allergens back to your home.

Most buildings experience similar challenges with allergen entry and air quality.

Air Quality Testing and Monitoring: Know What You’re Breathing

When it comes to protecting your family’s health, knowing what’s in your indoor air is just as important as keeping your home clean. Many Georgia residents are surprised to learn that poor indoor air quality can be a hidden culprit behind allergy symptoms, respiratory issues, and even more serious health problems like heart disease and lung cancer. That’s why air quality testing and monitoring are essential steps in creating a healthier indoor environment.

The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the World Health Organization (WHO) both stress the importance of regular air quality testing to identify indoor air pollutants that can affect your well-being. Indoor air pollution can come from a variety of sources—secondhand smoke, heating systems, household chemicals, and even outdoor air pollution that sneaks in through open windows or ventilation systems. In fact, outdoor air pollutants can easily make their way inside, especially during allergy season when pollen counts are high.

Air purifiers equipped with HEPA filters are a great way to remove harmful particles and fine particulate matter from your indoor spaces, but it’s just as important to identify and address the root causes of indoor air pollution. Proper ventilation is key to bringing in fresh air and preventing the buildup of indoor pollutants, but it’s a balancing act—too much outside air can introduce new pollutants, while inadequate ventilation can trap harmful chemicals and particles inside.

Damp indoor environments are another major concern, as they can lead to mold growth and increase the risk of health hazards. Mold assessment and removal, along with regular duct cleaning, are crucial for maintaining clean air and preventing mold exposure. Plumbing leaks and high humidity can create the perfect conditions for mold, so it’s important to address these issues promptly.

Professional air quality testing can detect a wide range of indoor air pollutants, including asbestos-containing materials, fine particulate matter, and even thirdhand smoke—the residue left behind by tobacco products. These tests can also uncover hidden problems like plumbing leaks that contribute to poor air quality. The National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences recommends regular air quality testing to safeguard human health, especially in sensitive environments like medical facilities and nursing homes, where excellent air quality is a top priority.

Long-term exposure to poor indoor air quality can have serious consequences, from aggravating allergies to increasing the risk of chronic health conditions. That’s why it’s so important to take proactive steps to improve indoor air quality—whether that means investing in air purification, scheduling duct cleaning, or simply ensuring your home has proper ventilation.

Don’t wait until allergy symptoms or health concerns arise. Take control of your indoor environment by scheduling a free consultation with a trusted air quality service provider. With professional air quality testing and tailored solutions, you can breathe easier knowing your home is a safe, healthy space for you and your loved ones.

Proven Methods to Reduce Allergens Indoors

Many allergy sufferers try to make do with portable air filters but these have their limitations as they can only clean a small amount of square footage per unit. The best option is to consult with indoor air quality (IAQ) specialists like the ones on our team.

We can install a whole house air purification system that’ll clean ALL the air that circulates throughout your entire home. For example, the AprilAire whole-home air purifiers equipped with HEPA filters are especially effective at capturing and removing airborne allergens such as mold spores, dust, pollen, and viruses. House plants can also help reduce certain indoor pollutants, but they should be chosen carefully to avoid mold growth or triggering allergies. Removing mold is an important step in reducing health risks associated with poor indoor air quality.

Don’t suffer through our extended allergy season any longer. Contact us today to learn about IAQ solutions.

During the summer months, maintaining good indoor air quality becomes especially important as homes are often closed up to keep cool air inside. Increased human activities such as cooking and cleaning during summer can contribute to indoor air pollution by releasing volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and other pollutants into the air.

Frequently Asked Questions

Question: What is the main focus of the article “Get Ahead of Georgia’s Allergy Season”?
Answer: The article explains how indoor allergens such as pollen, dust, mold spores, and pet dander can enter homes through HVAC intake vents, open doors or windows, and leaky ducts. It recommends taking steps to improve indoor air quality to reduce allergy triggers.

Question: Why might portable air filters not be enough to protect against allergies?
Answer: Portable air filters can clean only a limited area, usually a single room, so they may not address allergens circulating throughout the whole home. The article suggests a whole home air purification system for full house protection.

Question: What kind of air purification solution does the article recommend for allergy relief?
Answer: The article recommends installing a whole house air purification system, for example a system like those from AprilAire, to capture and remove common indoor allergens such as dust, pollen, mold spores, and even viruses throughout your entire home’s air circulation.

Question: What should homeowners do if they want to get ahead of allergy season according to the article?
Answer: Homeowners should consider consulting indoor air quality specialists to assess their home’s ventilation and HVAC system, and install a whole home air purification system to significantly reduce allergens.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for indoor air quality services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover how our air purifier services in Winder, GA can help you combat allergies not just in spring, but all year long.

item.title: Is Inverter AC Worth It In Georgia?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Many homeowners who live in challenging climates like ours here in Winder, GA want to know if upgrading to a more advanced AC system is worth it. One of these types of advanced systems is an inverter air conditioning system. We can say without a doubt that for combatting humidity and high temperatures, you can’t go wrong with inverter technology.

So what makes inverter ACs so special? We’re glad you asked! AC systems with inverter technology provide many benefits compared to standard, traditional AC systems. Let’s go over 5 benefits of these systems and how they’re the best bet for achieving unparalleled levels of performance and home comfort.

1. Energy Efficiency

A standard AC system has two speeds: on and off. But an inverter AC system has multiple speeds and settings. Not only that, but it can “choose” which variable speed to operate at to enhance performance. 

When the AC compressor doesn’t need to constantly run at full power when the system is on, the AC system uses less energy and runs more efficiently. That’s why inverter ACs can save significant amounts of energy compared to non-inverter models, leading to lower utility costs. 

2. Extended Lifespan

Running efficiently and less wear and tear equates to a longer system lifespan. Because inverter air conditioners need to operate at high and low speeds, the materials used in their components must adhere to a higher standard compared to their non-inverter counterparts. 

These technological advancements may include better materials, improved designs, and enhanced control systems, which can contribute to increased durability and longevity.

3.  Better Cooling Prowess

An AC system with inverter technology can cool your home faster than a standard AC system. The variable speed compressor allows the system to ramp up quickly and slow down when it approaches the set temperature, providing faster, more efficient cooling power when it’s needed.

4. Better for Planet Earth

If you pride yourself on making an eco-friendly choice, an inverter AC system is the way to go. You can feel happy about the fact that you’ve chosen one of the “greenest” AC systems on the market. The variable-speed processor is what allows these systems to operate super efficiently, making them the eco-friendly option.

5. Quieter Operation

Part of your home comfort is enjoying your home. AC inverters operate more quietly than their standard counterparts. Because the compressor doesn’t need to start and stop frequently at full power, there are fewer abrupt fluctuations in noise levels, leading to a quieter overall operation.

If you’re a homeowner who cares about the environment or just wants to decrease your cooling costs, ask one of our experts if an inverter AC system is right for your home and budget. No matter what air conditioning system you use for your home, you need a system that fits your needs so be sure to contact one of our professionals.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for AC services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: AC systems with inverter technology provide many benefits compared to standard AC systems such as greater energy efficiency and reduced cooling costs

item.title: Common AC Issues When You Turn It On in Spring
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When you turn your air conditioner on for the first time after not using it for a while, there are some common issues you may experience from not using it for a few months. Of course, some DIY maintenance and scheduling a thorough tune-up from our team can help avoid these problems, but you may still experience some issues.

Let’s go over some common problems that you may run into so you’ll know if you need to call us for air conditioning repair in Winder, GA. 

Why Your AC Might Not Start This Spring

As you make the switch from using your HVAC system for cooling to heating, when it seems like the AC won’t turn on, the first thing you might think is that your furnace is actually the problem. But this isn’t necessarily the case.

First of all, check to make sure your thermostat has been changed from “heating” to “cooling” or the AC setting. Also, check the batteries in the thermostat. Make sure you’ve lowered the thermostat enough to ensure it kicks on. If your AC still won’t turn on, try resetting the circuit breaker. If none of these troubleshooting efforts help, give us a call.

AC Too Slow? Here’s Why It Can’t Reach the Right Temp

If it seems like it’s taking forever for your AC to reach the set temperature, this could be due to many different issues. The first thing you want to do is make sure your AC has a clean filter. It’s best to start off the cooling season with a nice clean filter to make sure your AC has strong airflow throughout the unit.

The next thing to check is the evaporator coils to make sure they are clean and free of any dirt, dust, and debris. When they’re dirty, it will be nearly impossible for your AC to reach the set temperature. Try vacuuming them with a dry brush attachment. If that doesn’t do the trick, we’ll give them a professional cleaning during an annual maintenance checkup.

Weak Airflow or Lukewarm Air? Spring AC Issues Explained

The air that comes from your AC when you first turn it on should be decidedly cold and unmistakeably strong. A lack of cold air or strong airflow could be due to a dirty air filter. If you’ve already replaced this, check the condensing unit. This is the outdoor component of your AC. 

During winter, it’s common for your condensing unit to become dirty or for branches, twigs, and leaves to accumulate on the unit. Clear away any debris from the unit and make sure no grass has grown up high around it. If it’s exceptionally dirty, try rinsing it off gently with a garden hose. 

Is your AC still not working at optimal performance? Give us a call to schedule an appointment for maintenance to make sure your system is set up to deliver cooling power that’ll keep you comfortable through spring and summer.

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for air conditioning repair in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn common AC problems when restarting in spring. Avoid surprise repairs and keep your system running smoothly.

item.title: 7 Signs Your Commercial Heating System Needs Help
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Commercial heating systems are tasked with the enormous responsibility of keeping tenants, customers, and employees safe, warm, and comfortable. If you’re a commercial property owner, it’s important to be aware of the signs that your commercial heating system is plagued by issues leading to sub-par performance. 

As a business owner, you have a legal responsibility to your tenants and employees to promptly fix any issues with a commercial heating system. Count on us to prioritize a struggling commercial HVAC system that isn’t performing at optimum efficiency. Luckily, commercial HVAC systems are similar to residential heating systems in that the signs they give off when they’re struggling with performance are similar. Let’s go over 7 of these signs.   

1. Cold or Lukewarm Air

The air that flows from a commercial heating system should be hot, not lukewarm or cold. This issue might best be caught in the form of increased heating bills as the system struggles to heat the commercial space or complaints from tenants about the building taking a long time to heat up.

2. Weak Airflow

On the other hand, if the air is hot but the airflow is low, something is impeding the system’s ability to generate strong and steady airflow. Changing the system’s air filters regularly is a task we take care of during commercial HVAC maintenance. If you’ve skipped this service, weak airflow could be the result.

3. Uneven Heating

One of the easiest signs to recognize is inconsistent heating throughout the office building, apartment complex, or business. Certain areas may feel significantly colder or warmer than others, indicating potential issues with ductwork, vents, or the heating system itself.

4. Increased Energy Consumption

A malfunctioning heating system often requires more energy to maintain the desired temperature, leading to a noticeable increase in energy bills for the office or business. Monitoring energy usage and identifying unexplained spikes can help detect potential problems with the heating system that require repair.

5. Strange Odors

Strange odors can be hard to detect in commercial spaces such as restaurants, warehouses, and production plants. That’s why you should pay prompt attention to any reports of strange, sudden odors. Burning smells or musty odors indicate issues such as overheating components, mold growth, or debris accumulation within the system. It’s essential to address unusual odors quickly before they escalate into major problems such as an electrical fire or a mold infestation.

6. Poor Indoor Air Quality

A commercial heating system in need of TLC can cause poor indoor air quality within the commercial space. This can negatively affect people who have allergies, respiratory issues, or discomfort among occupants. Issues such as dirty filters, mold growth, or pollutants circulating through the system require repair to restore optimal air quality.

7. Visible Signs of Damage

Periodically make a visual inspection of your commercial HVAC equipment, looking for physical signs of damage or deterioration such as leaks, rust, and corrosion. These are signs of potential issues that need to be addressed promptly before they become major(and costly) problems to fix.  

Contact American Comfort Heating and Cooling for commercial heating services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: If you’re a commercial property owner, it’s important to be aware of the signs that your commercial heating system is plagued by issues leading to sub-par performance.

item.title: Yes, Tankless Water Heaters Need Maintenance Too
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Homeowners who have made the switch to a tankless water heater from a traditional tank water heater are undoubtedly happy with its performance. Also called “on-demand” water heaters, their benefits include an unlimited supply of hot water and lower utility bills.

However, there’s still one thing that tankless water heaters require, same as tank water heaters. And that one thing is maintenance. Don’t be fooled into thinking your new tankless system is so easy and “low maintenance” that it doesn’t need tune-ups. It does. Here’s why maintenance is just as important for tankless water heaters as it is for tank water heaters.

Why does a water heater need maintenance?

Water heaters need maintenance just like any piece of mechanical equipment like a car, furnace, or air conditioner. Tankless water heaters require annual maintenance to ensure their functionality. 

Think about how much your tankless water heater installation cost when you purchased it. Maintenance is the best way to protect that substantial investment. Scheduling water heater maintenance will help increase the lifespan of your system, protect the warranty, and ensure your water heater has a long service life.

What happens during tankless water heater maintenance? 

During a maintenance service, one of our technicians will perform a thorough examination of your tankless water heater. One of the most important tasks is to check it for hard water deposits.

In Winder, GA, our water is somewhat hard. That means that it has excessive amounts of minerals. Although these minerals aren’t harmful to drink, they can build up inside both tankless and tank water heaters, ultimately interfering with their performance. This is called scaling. 

One of the main things that your technician will check for is whether the water heater needs descaling and flushing out the unit. This involves removing any mineral deposits that can disrupt its operation. They’ll also check the filters, the water pressure, the temperature, and other steps that will make sure your tankless water heater is in top-notch condition.

How often does my tankless water heater need maintenance?

Tankless water heater maintenance is recommended once a year. If your tankless water heater is under warranty, be sure to check the fine print to determine the requirements for maintenance. 

Can I perform tankless water heater maintenance on my own?

Homeowners should not attempt to maintain their water heaters. Not only will doing so void the warranty, but you could also damage the water heater–and yourself. Water heaters are high-pressure systems that hold boiling-hot water. The risk of injury or damage to the water heater is high if you attempt a DIY tune-up or flushing.

Our water heater services are always fast, affordable, and efficient so don’t hesitate to call and schedule with our conscientious team. We service all types of water heaters including conventional tank water heaters, tankless water heaters, and heat pump water heaters.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater service in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Don’t be fooled into thinking your new tankless system is so easy and “low maintenance” that it doesn’t need tune-ups. It does.

item.title: What&#8217;s the Difference Between Ducted &#038; Ductless Heat Pumps?
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
As more and more homeowners are making the switch to heat pumps, there are still people who aren’t aware of their benefits. Perhaps you’ve tried researching them and your initial results have left you with more questions than answers.

We understand that heat pump pumps can be a bit confusing. That’s why we’re going to go over the differences in this blog. Keep reading to learn more. And feel free to call us with any questions about heat pumps or the heat pump service we provide. We’re happy to explain the differences and recommend a system based on your needs.

The Name “Heat pump” Can Be Confusing

Here in Georgia, with our hot and humid summers, many people become confused by the name “heat pump” when they search for air conditioning options. Heat pumps got that name because they work by pumping heat from one location to another. But rest assured that a heat pump will provide both air conditioning and heating.

What’s the Difference Between Ducted & Ductless Heat Pumps?

You may have also seen references to ductless heat pumps and ducted heat pumps. So what’s the difference? In this case, the names are appropriate. Here’s the difference:

  • Ductless heat pumps don’t require ductwork to be installed and to work. 
  • Ducted heat pumps do require ductwork. 

It helps to think of a ducted heat pump as having a similar setup to a central AC: there’s an indoor unit and an outdoor unit. You could even call a central AC ducted AC if that helps!

So What’s a Mini Split?

Here’s another term causing confusion among homeowners. But having read this far, it’ll help you to know that mini splits are also called “ductless mini split systems.” So, a mini split is simply a ductless heat pump. 

Which System Is Best for Me?

Well, the first thing to think about when choosing between systems is your ductwork! If your home doesn’t have ductwork, going with a ductless mini split is a much better option because you won’t have to pay to have ductwork installed.

If your home is old and your ductwork is full of holes and tears, then ductless is the way to go. But if your home is newer and your ductwork is in great shape, then a ducted heat pump might be the better choice.

Keep in mind that a mini split system will be more expensive to purchase and installed. With a ducted heat pump, all we need to do is install the indoor and outdoor units. 

However, a mini split system requires installing individual air handlers on multiple rooms and zones of your home. It’s also a lot more equipment to purchase upfront which is why the initial purchase price can be higher. 

We hope we’ve cleared up the confusing surrounding “heat pumps.” Contact us with any lingering questions you may have.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heat pump service in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: If you've been researching heat pumps and your initial results have left you with more questions than answers, we're here to help!

item.title: 3 Signs You Need Water Heater Repair
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When your home’s water heater is working as it should, you might not appreciate the fact that it provides you with hot water 24/7.

But at the first signs of problems, you’ll find yourself missing how well it performed. The signs that something is amiss can be subtle or they can be obvious. Keep reading to know when it’s time to call us for prompt water heater repair.

1. Not Enough Hot Water

Is there anything worse than not having enough hot water to complete basic activities throughout the day? When it’s cold outside, the last thing you want to deal with is a hot shower that turns icy cold after a few minutes, or you notice that your clothes don’t seem to be getting clean enough when you run a load on hot.

A lack of hot water could be caused by several different issues. If the lack of hot water only occurs in one showerhead or tap, the mixing valve could be broken. However, if you’ve noticed a lack of hot water throughout your entire home, it’s the water heater.

Water heaters struggle with efficiency as they reach the end of their lifespans. This results in its inability to heat enough water. If your home suffers from hard water, this can wreak havoc on a water heater. When mineral deposits form, these hard water deposits can interfere with the water heater’s heating functionality.

2. No Hot Water at All

If you’re not getting hot water from any taps in your home, this is a situation that needs to be remedied quickly. A complete lack of hot water is a health and safety issue. Your home requires hot water to clean and sterilize dishes, wash your hands, and for daily bathing and grooming. 

If you have a gas water heater, it could be an issue with the thermocouple. The thermocouple is a safety component that monitors gas flow into the combustion chamber and can wear down over time. Whatever the reason, a complete lack of hot water requires prompt attention from our team. 

3. Reduced Water Pressure

A reduction in water pressure can be harder to determine, especially if it comes on slowly. You may notice that the water pressure is lower with only the hot water vs the cold water, and vice versa. But once you’re certain the pressure has decreased, check with your neighbors and see if they’re experiencing the issue. It could be a problem with the water supplier.

Decreased water pressure can stem from a water heater problem due to hard water. As sediment from the mineral deposits builds up, they can impede how much water passes through the water heater. 

It could also be caused by the water heater’s shut-off valve. The shut-off valve on a water heater restricts the flow of water from the pipe. It’s possible that it was moved by accident by you or a family member. 

Frequently Asked Questions

Question: What are the signs my water heater isn’t producing enough hot water?
Answer: If your hot water runs out too quickly during showers or doesn’t get hot enough for cleaning, especially throughout your home, not just one outlet, it might indicate mineral sediment buildup, aging components, or an inefficient heater struggling with hard water issues.

Question: Why am I getting no hot water at all from my taps?
Answer: A total lack of hot water is a clear warning that something’s wrong, particularly in gas units, where a failed thermocouple (a safety device monitoring gas flow) could be the culprit. This scenario requires prompt attention for both comfort and safety.

Question: Why is my hot water flow weak compared to cold water?
Answer: Reduced hot water pressure, especially if cold water pressure remains normal, can point to sediment clogged inside your heater or a mishandled shut-off valve restricting flow. This often occurs gradually, making it easy to overlook.

Question: When should I call a professional for water heater repair?
Answer: You should call a professional if you notice inconsistent water temperatures, no hot water at all, or a significant drop in hot water pressure. These are common signs of internal problems that won’t resolve on their own and could worsen without expert repair.

Question: Can hard water damage my water heater?
Answer: Yes, hard water often leads to mineral and sediment buildup inside your water heater. Over time, this reduces heating efficiency, restricts water flow, and shortens the unit’s lifespan. Regular flushing and maintenance can help prevent costly repairs.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater repair in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The signs that something is amiss can be subtle or they can be obvious. Keep reading to know when it’s time to call us for prompt water heater repair.

item.title: Why Your Heat Pump Isn&#8217;t Switching Modes Anymore
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
If you own a heat pump in Winder, GA, you undoubtedly appreciate its versatility when it comes to our unpredictable weather. You may find that you’re switching from heating to cooling mode and vice versa from one day to the next with our extreme temperature fluctuations.

So it can be incredibly frustrating when your heat pump won’t switch modes anymore. Although there’s one thing you can try that might solve this issue, it’s usually a problem that’s big enough to warrant heat pump repair. Here’s what could be going wrong.

The Air Filter Needs to Be Changed

This is the potential cause behind a heat pump that’s not blowing decidedly hot or cold air. When the airflow is lukewarm, it could seem like the heat pump isn’t switching modes. You’ll also notice that the airflow is decreased or weak if it’s an air filter issue.

If you’re great at changing the air filter every 1-2 months, it’s not likely going to be the issue. But if your mind is saying, “When’s the last time I changed the air filter?” this could be the problem. 

Check the air filter and give it a visual inspection. A new air filter looks, well, new and clean. If yours is grey, dusty, dirty, and covered with gunk, put a new one in then see if that fixes the problem.

The Reversing Valve Is Broken

Your heat pump’s ability to switch from heating to cooling and vice versa hinges on this essential component that’s only found in heat pumps. The aptly named reversing valve allows the heat pump to “reverse” the flow of refrigerant. This reversal is how the heat pump switches modes.

If your heat pump won’t switch modes and is blowing cold air in heating mode, or hot air in cooling mode, 9 times out of 10, it’s because of the reversing valve. However, this isn’t a DIY fix. You’ll need to schedule service with one of our technicians who will inspect the reversing valve, determine if it can be fixed, or replace it with a new one if necessary.

There’s a Refrigerant Leak

This is another sneaky culprit that’ll make it seem like a heat pump isn’t switching modes when really it’s just struggling to both heat and cool. Just like with the air filter, the symptoms are lukewarm air and decreased airflow.

A heat pump works like an air conditioner and needs a full charge of refrigerant to operate. Just like AC systems, heat pumps are subject to refrigerant leaks for the same reasons they occur in a central AC. Wear and tear, corrosion, and damage can cause a leak.

If this is the problem, we’ll need to pinpoint the sources of the leaks. We’ll patch them up and recharge the refrigerant level. Then your heat pump should continue to provide unparalleled heating and cooling in your home no matter the season.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for heat pump repair in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: A heat pump that won't switch from heating to cooling and vice versa is frustrating indeed. Here are some causes behind this issue.

item.title: 7 Sounds That Indicate Your Furnace Needs Repairs ASAP
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Being a homeowner is no easy task. No matter the season, it seems like there’s a never-ending list of things on the to-do list. If “furnace repair” is on that list, here’s why you should check that one off sooner rather than later.

It’s crucial to schedule necessary repairs as soon as you notice any issues to prevent further damage and avoid more expensive replacements down the line. Furnace repairs are necessary to restore system performance completely, ensuring your home remains comfortable and safe.

Ignoring furnace issues can cause something that might be an easy fix to escalate into a bigger problem. If furnace breaks occur, there is a risk of dangerous situations such as carbon monoxide leaks, which can pose serious health risks. Even worse, you could find yourself with a completely broken down furnace when a bout of cold weather is in the forecast. It’s important to address furnace maintenance before cooler weather arrives to ensure your comfort and prevent unexpected breakdowns. When system fails happen due to neglected maintenance, you may be left without heat and face costly repairs. Here are some noises that your furnace will make when it’s letting you know it needs some help. 

What are signs your furnace needs a tune-up?

Unusual furnace noises often signal the need for immediate maintenance. Common warning sounds include loud booming or rumbling during startup, constant clicking, high-pitched squealing or screeching, metal-on-metal rattling, and air hissing. These indicate issues with ignition, belts, motors, or airflow that require professional attention.

It’s important to know the difference between a furnace repair and a tune-up: a repair addresses urgent problems or breakdowns, while a tune-up focuses on routine maintenance to prevent issues. Tune-ups are routine services during which technicians inspect your HVAC system and replace parts as needed. Recognizing these signs early helps homeowners make informed decisions about whether they need a repair or a tune-up.

Introduction to Furnace Repair

When the temperatures drop and cold weather sets in, your heating system becomes the heart of your home’s comfort. That’s why furnace repair and regular furnace tune ups are so important. Regular furnace maintenance is essential for system reliability and longevity. A well maintained furnace not only keeps your home warm and cozy, but it also helps you avoid costly repairs and unexpected breakdowns that can leave you in the cold. Regular maintenance and tune ups ensure your furnace operates at peak efficiency, reducing energy consumption and helping you save money on utility bills. Furnace tune ups are important for maintaining energy efficiency and preventing emergency repairs that can be both inconvenient and expensive. By scheduling routine furnace tune ups, you extend the lifespan of your heating system and keep it running smoothly all season long. Regular maintenance extends the lifespan of furnaces, providing reliable heating for years. Don’t wait for a breakdown, investing in regular maintenance is the best way to enjoy reliable heat, lower energy bills, and peace of mind throughout the winter.

As a local business with years of experience serving the Metro Atlanta area, we are committed to honest communication, responsive service, and delivering great work at a great price. Our owner is directly involved in every aspect of the business, ensuring customer satisfaction and upholding our reputation for integrity. We provide comprehensive heating and air services, including professional installation, repair, and maintenance. American Comfort is highly rated on Yelp with quick response times and verified licenses. Whether you need to install a new system or require urgent furnace repair, our team is here to help. Scheduling a service call with us ensures you receive expert installation or necessary repairs, and we always discuss your options openly. Remember, delaying repairs can mean you may have to pay more for expensive replacement parts or additional damage, so prompt attention is key to keeping your HVAC system running efficiently.

Types of Heating Systems

Understanding the different types of heating systems available can help you make informed decisions that save you money on energy bills and prevent costly repairs down the road.

The most common heating systems found in Atlanta, GA, homes include gas furnaces, electric heat pumps, dual fuel systems, and high-efficiency models. Gas furnaces are a popular choice for their dependable heating and ability to quickly warm your home, making them ideal for chilly winter nights. Electric heat pumps, on the other hand, are known for their energy efficiency and environmental benefits, as they can both heat and cool your home while keeping energy bills in check. For homeowners seeking flexibility and optimal performance, dual fuel systems combine the strengths of both gas and electric heating, automatically switching between the two to maximize efficiency and comfort.

High-efficiency heating systems are designed to minimize energy consumption, reduce your carbon footprint, and help you avoid unnecessary repairs. No matter which system you choose, regular tune ups and proper care are crucial for maintaining peak performance and extending the life of your equipment. Experienced technicians with specialized training can guide you through the process of selecting, installing, and maintaining the right heating system for your home, ensuring you receive excellent customer service and dependable results.

Investing in the right heating system and scheduling regular maintenance with knowledgeable professionals is the best way to enjoy reliable comfort, lower energy bills, and peace of mind throughout the winter. If you’re considering purchasing a new system or need service for your current unit, don’t hesitate to contact a trusted company in Atlanta for expert advice and support.

Importance of Regular Maintenance

Regular maintenance is the foundation of a reliable and efficient heating system. Scheduling routine furnace tune ups is one of the best ways to ensure your furnace operates at peak efficiency, especially when cold weather arrives. A well maintained furnace not only keeps your home comfortable, but it also helps reduce energy consumption, leading to lower energy bills and a smaller environmental footprint. By investing in annual maintenance, you can catch small issues before they turn into costly repairs or emergency furnace repair situations, saving you money and stress in the long run.

Regular furnace tune ups also play a crucial role in maintaining indoor air quality. Dust, debris, and other contaminants can build up in your system, affecting the air you breathe and potentially triggering allergies or respiratory issues. As part of annual maintenance, it is important to regularly clean or replace air filters to ensure optimal system performance and keep your indoor air fresh and healthy. Maintenance visits include cleaning and inspecting components, which helps keep your indoor air fresh and healthy. Additionally, regular maintenance reduces the risk of carbon monoxide leaks, ensuring your family’s safety and providing peace of mind.

Most furnace manufacturers require regular maintenance to keep your warranty valid, so staying on top of tune ups protects your investment. With accurate temperature control and fewer unexpected breakdowns, a well maintained furnace operates smoothly throughout the heating season. Ultimately, regular maintenance means optimal performance, lower utility bills, and the confidence that your heating system is ready for whatever winter brings.

Furnace Components and Maintenance

Understanding the key components of your furnace can help you appreciate the importance of regular maintenance. The heat exchanger is at the heart of your furnace, transferring heat safely and efficiently. Regular heat exchanger inspection is essential to detect cracks or damage that could lead to carbon monoxide leaks or reduced performance. The blower motor is responsible for circulating warm air throughout your home; keeping it clean and well-lubricated ensures proper airflow and prevents overheating. Timely heating repair and heating system repair are essential to prevent system failures and avoid the need for costly replacement of major components.

The gas valve and flue pipe are critical for safe operation. The gas valve controls the flow of natural gas, while the flue pipe vents combustion gases safely outside. Regular maintenance checks for leaks or blockages in these components, helping to prevent dangerous gas leaks and carbon monoxide exposure. The air filter is another vital part, replacing it regularly improves indoor air quality, reduces energy consumption, and helps your furnace run more efficiently.

During a professional furnace tune up, technicians will also inspect the thermostat settings, pilot light, and combustion chamber to ensure everything is working as it should. These checks help maintain accurate temperature control and prevent issues like uneven heating or system failures. If you have a heat pump, regular maintenance is just as important, as these systems provide both heating and cooling. Well maintained heat pumps operate at peak performance, keeping your home comfortable year-round while reducing energy consumption.

By taking care of each component through regular furnace tune ups and maintenance, you can extend the lifespan of your heating system, avoid major repairs, and enjoy optimal performance throughout the heating season.

Knocking and Rattling

Your furnace is a complicated piece of equipment. As such, it’s composed of many pieces and parts, some of which can come loose every now and then. 

The sound of something knocking or rattling about is probably just that. Some screws could’ve come loose and are causing metal parts to jostle about. It could be a loose screw that’s come completely loose and is rattling around inside. Or it could be a loose blower fan banging against other parts. Loose or faulty electrical connections can also cause knocking or rattling noises in the furnace.

Squealing

A squealing sound usually indicates that the fan belt is coming loose. It’ll sound similar to the noise your car makes when it has a loose belt. You don’t want the fan belt to break entirely so give us a call if you hear this noise. We can tighten the belt or replace it if necessary.

Screeching

Your furnace is composed of many metal parts. The day it was installed, these parts had plenty of lubrication between them that allowed your furnace to run smoothly, efficiently, and quietly.

If you hear a screeching, grinding, or metal-on-metal noise, it usually means that the lubrication has worn down. We see this often in furnaces that haven’t received regular tune-ups. Buildup or debris in the combustion chamber can also contribute to screeching or grinding noises, so regular cleaning of the combustion chamber is important for smooth operation.

Booming and Rumbling

Do you own a gas furnace? Booming and rumbling are of particular concern. Although it happens rarely, explosions have been known to happen.

A gas furnace uses a combustion process to light the gas burners. Issues with the gas line, such as a closed or damaged gas line, can disrupt the combustion process and lead to safety concerns. Sometimes this process can be impeded by anything from a dirty burner to an ignition issue. When the ignition process is delayed, the booming or rumbling sound results. You do not want the mini-explosion in your furnace to become a bigger explosion. You do not want the mini-explosion in your furnace to become a bigger explosion.

Delayed ignition or combustion issues can also increase the risk of carbon monoxide leaks, making prompt professional inspection essential for your safety. Call us promptly for booming and rumbling sounds in a gas furnace.

Clicking

Another gas furnace issue is a repeated clicking noise. This sounds similar to the noise your stovetop makes when it takes multiple attempts to light the gas burner. This noise is caused by a problem with the ignition process either due to the pilot light or the thermocouple. 

Clicking noises should be addressed right away as again, this indicates a problem with the gas combustion process in your gas furnace. With safety in mind, give us a call to diagnose and fix a clicking furnace problem. Homeowners should also regularly check their carbon monoxide detectors to ensure they are functioning properly, especially when experiencing ignition issues.

DIY vs Professional Furnace Maintenance

While there are a few simple steps homeowners can take to keep their furnace running efficiently, like regularly changing the air filter, checking thermostat settings, and making sure vents are clear, many aspects of furnace maintenance are best left to the professionals. Tasks such as heat exchanger inspection, checking the gas valve and gas lines, inspecting the blower motor, and ensuring proper operation of the flue pipe require specialized knowledge and tools. During a furnace tune-up, professionals will inspect critical components like burners and heat exchangers. Attempting DIY repairs on these critical components can lead to safety hazards, including gas leaks or carbon monoxide exposure, and may even void your furnace’s warranty. Professional HVAC companies offer comprehensive furnace tune ups that include safety inspections, cleaning, and adjustments to ensure your heating system operates at peak performance. These companies are also experts in air conditioning and heating air services, providing installation, repair, and maintenance for both systems to ensure year-round comfort and efficient climate control. Regular HVAC system maintenance is essential for overall home comfort and energy efficiency. HVAC tune ups should be scheduled seasonally to ensure both your heating and cooling systems are ready for peak usage. Many companies offer a one time tune up as a preventive service for homeowners who want to make sure their system is in top shape before the season starts. Maintaining the cooling system is just as important as the heating system for year-round comfort. Inspecting the outdoor ac unit during maintenance helps prevent issues and ensures optimal system performance. Most furnace manufacturers recommend annual maintenance by certified technicians to maintain optimal efficiency, improve indoor air quality, and avoid major repairs. Trusting your annual furnace tune up to a professional not only protects your investment but also gives you peace of mind knowing your home’s heating is safe and comfortable.

Conclusion and Next Steps

Your furnace is essential for keeping your home comfortable and safe during the heating season. If you notice any unusual noises or signs of trouble, don’t ignore them, addressing issues early can help you avoid costly repairs and emergency furnace repair when you need heat the most. Emergency and same-day heating repair services are available to restore comfort quickly in case of sudden heating failures. Scheduling regular furnace tune ups and annual maintenance with a trusted HVAC company is the best way to ensure your heating system operates efficiently, reduces energy consumption, and provides reliable warmth all winter long. It’s important to maintain all types of heating systems to ensure optimal comfort and safety in your home. During maintenance, be sure to have components like condenser coils and ductwork inspected to prevent performance issues and maintain good indoor air quality. Scheduling a furnace tune-up in early fall is ideal for addressing potential issues before winter. Take the next step toward a well maintained furnace by booking your annual tune up today. With professional care, you’ll enjoy lower energy bills, improved indoor air quality, and the peace of mind that comes from knowing your furnace is ready for whatever the weather brings.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for furnace repair in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: From booming and banging, to squealing and screeching, here are 7 furnace noises that require a prompt call for repairs.

item.title: Water Heaters &#038; Efficiency Ratings
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
When it’s time for water heater replacement in Winder, GA, there are two major decisions you’ll have to make: if you get a tank or tankless water heater, and your budget.

Tankless water heaters generally cost more upfront but use less energy over the lifespan of the system. Traditional tank water heaters cost less upfront but cost more to run for as long as you own it.

Another big factor in a water heater’s cost to run is its energy efficiency rating. A water heater that uses less energy will cost more and one that’s an energy hot will cost less. Let’s look at water heaters and energy usage in depth. 

Energy Star Ratings

The easiest way to choose a water heater that you know will be energy efficient is to look for the ENERGY STAR-certified label. The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) gives ENERGY STAR certification to water heaters that use less energy. 

The rating is based on the water heater’s uniform energy factor (UEF) rating which measures is based on two things: how much energy it uses and how much is used to power the water heater itself. The higher the UEF number, the more energy-efficient the water heater will be. To earn an ENERGY-STAR designation, water heaters must meet these criteria.

  • Gas tank water heater (up to 55 gallons) must have a minimum UEF of 0.81
  • Gas tank water heater ( 55+ gallons) must have a minimum UEF of 0.86
  • Gas tankless water heater must have a minimum UEF of 0.87

The Type of Water Heater & Energy Consumption

There are 2 types of water heater types: tankless and tank. Let’s go over their pros and cons solely in terms of energy efficiency. 

  • Tank water heaters are found in most homes in the US. They keep a “tank” of hot water ready at all times. This requires energy usage 24 hours a day as the water heater is constantly using energy to keep a supply of hot water available for use. Although they’re less energy efficient than tankless models, there are features you can choose to increase energy efficiency.
  • Tankless water heaters are also called on-demand water heaters because they heat water “on demand” without the use of a storage tank. Because water is heated only as you need it, they use a lot less energy. According to the DOE, they can be 24-34% more efficient than tank water heaters which would result in some impressive savings on monthly utility expenses.

If you need water heater services, you need a reliable team. Our technicians are licensed, knowledgeable, and experienced. In fact, we continually train our technicians so that they’re always up to date with the latest advancements in water heater technology. Trust the team that has served the Winder, GA area for the past 50 years.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for water heater replacement in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Let’s look at water heaters and energy usage in depth. That way, you'll know what to choose when it's time for a new water heater.

item.title: Most Common HVAC Repairs New Homeowners Should Know About
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
If you’re a new homeowner, congratulations! Although owning your home is a daunting undertaking, it’s a great investment in your future. As a new homeowner, it’s especially important to learn the basics of things to be on the lookout for regarding your home’s major systems. 

When it comes to your HVAC system, which is a fairly pricy piece of equipment, it’s essential to know the things that can go wrong. When money is tight after a new home purchase, it pays to invest the time in learning about ways to save money by being a savvy homeowner. Let’s go over the most common HVAC repairs you’ll probably have to deal with someday.

Air Filter Issues

The air filter is one of the most important components of your HVAC system. And the really good news is that this is an easy thing to take care of yourself. Simply replacing (or cleaning and reinstalling if you have a reusable air filter) the air filter will keep dust, dirt, dander, and debris out of your HVAC system’s internal working parts. It’s a bit like the filter in your car, only much more important. 

The failure to change the air filter every 1-3 months will result in a plethora of problems related to poor airflow. A clogged filter causes increased air resistance and lowers airflow. Your HVAC system will expend extra energy to heat and cool your home when it shouldn’t have to. It also decreased the lifespan of your system, meaning you’ll need to pay for an expensive replacement sooner than if you’d changed the filter regularly.

Malfunctioning Thermostat

The thermostat is the “brains” behind your HVAC system, controlling the temperature throughout your home. The thermostat can become miscalibrated and when it does, it can’t send the correct signals to your HVAC system. If your home has an outdated thermostat, consider replacing it with a smart/Wi-Fi thermostat that’ll give you even greater temperature control throughout your home.

Blown Fuses or Tripped Breakers

A common HVAC repair need is when the system keeps tripping breakers or blowing fuses in your home. This can happen when the blower starts working too hard, caused by airflow blockage due to a clogged filter. If you notice your HVAC system keeps tripping the breaker in your home, give us a call to repair the problem. 

Unusual Noises

Your HVAC system should function fairly silently. You’ll hear it turn on and off as well as the sound of air flowing from the vents. Other than that, you shouldn’t hear much else. If you ever hear sudden, loud, or unusual noises, the system is giving you a sign that it’s struggling. These sounds can include banging, booming, screeching, grinding, and the sound of metal on metal.

Short Cycling

This is a more unusual problem, but one that needs prompt attention. Short cycling occurs when the system frequently turns on and off and is unable to complete a heating or cooling cycle. Give us a call to investigate the cause and restore your home comfort.

Contact the team at American Comfort Heating and Cooling for HVAC services in Winder, GA. Bringing Comfort to America, One Home at a Time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: As a new homeowner, when it comes to your HVAC system, which is a fairly pricy piece of equipment, it’s essential to know the things that can go wrong.

item.title: What Happens When You Avoid Heating Maintenance
item.dc:creator: amerciancomfort
item.content:encoded:
Here in Georgia, homeowners rely less on their heating than on air conditioning. With our mild winters, heating system maintenance is a service you may be tempted to skip out on, thinking it’s not that necessary. But regular tune-ups are one of the most important heating services we provide.

It doesn’t matter if you have a central furnace, a heat pump, or a mini split to heat your home in the winter–they all need to be tuned up ideally in the fall before you start relying on them for heat. Let’s go over what can happen if you avoid scheduling this essential service. 

The heating system can pose a safety hazard.

This is especially important if you have a gas furnace. We don’t want to scare homeowners and although safety issues are rare, they can still happen. Even neglected electric furnaces pose a potential risk of a fire, especially if the system is old and outdated. 

During maintenance, the system is thoroughly inspected to make sure it’s working safely and efficiently with an eye for safety. In a gas furnace, the heat exchanger will be checked since over time, this component is susceptible to wear and tear. The electrical connections, wires, and fuses will be checked to make sure they’re operating properly. Maintenance should put your mind at ease that your heating system is operating safely.

The heating system will become unreliable.

During the holidays and once the cold weather really sets in, the last thing you want to worry about is a heating system that isn’t working reliably. Although there’s no foolproof method to avoid breakdowns, maintenance can prevent up to 80% of breakdowns and repair needs, which is significant. That means less inconvenience as well as less money paid to keep your system running. Don’t run the risk of not having a working heating system when you need it most.

The heating system will waste money.

On average, a heating system will lose 5% of its energy efficiency for every year that it misses maintenance. The longer the system doesn’t receive maintenance, the more its performance plummets. Annual maintenance helps prevent this efficiency loss. 

And when your system isn’t running efficiently, you’ll feel in in your home comfort levels and see it on your utility bills which will slowly keep climbing as you run your system longer to achieve previous results. 

The heating system will need an early replacement.

Most heating systems have an estimated lifespan of 10-15 years. However, this age range only applies when the system receives regular scheduled maintenance. In fact, skipping out on maintenance can void the warranty. 

The best way to help your system last for 10-15 years is to schedule maintenance. Tune-ups keep your system operating at peak performance and efficiency. Without this service, wear and tear will take their toll, shortening the lifespan of the system. Maintenance easily pays for itself this way!
